Title: Graduate Program Data Science & Analytics - Italy
Description:
Job Description

Are you looking to kick off your career journey?

Do you like the idea of joining the European PayTech?

If so, you are at the right place!

Our Graduate Program offers you the chance to create your unique career path within our diverse international team, operating in more than 25 countries. Rotate among different departments, learn from experienced professionals, and make a difference by working on real business cases.

About The Program

Our Graduate Program is more than a learning path, it's a journey where you will work on real business cases and have a direct impact on business results.

What Can You Expect?


Career Path: Start with a permanent contract from day one. Over 12 months, experience two rotations across departments and transition into a landing role where you can continue to grow.
Training & Development: Receive a well-structured, dedicated Project Management training program, tailored to strengthen your ability to lead projects and deliver results. Plus, gain access to a wide range of learning opportunities through our Digital PayTech University, empowering you to continuously build your skills and expand your knowledge.
Impact & Networking: Contribute to real business solutions and expand your network by working alongside talented colleagues and senior leaders from across Europe.


Who are We Looking For?

If this sounds like you, we encourage you to apply:


You have graduated within the past 12 months or will graduate within the next 6 months with a master’s degree in Engineering, STEM disciplines, Economics, Business, Law, or related fields, and are available to work full-time.
You are fluent in English and Italian.
You bring international experience, whether through study, work abroad, or collaboration in a global environment.
You are a career starter with no more than one year of professional experience.
You have the legal right to work in Italy (e.g., residency, visa).
You are committed, curious, and eager to take ownership of projects while thriving in a team environment.
You see formal training as just the beginning and aim to make a difference in everything you do.


Diversity, Equity and Inclusion

We are committed to building a diverse culture where talents from all walks of life feel they truly belong. By applying to our Graduate Program, you are one step closer to joining us. Next starts with you, everyday.

Apply Now!

Don't miss out on this amazing opportunity. Apply today and discover what's in it for you!

Please ensure both your CV and all answers in the application form are completed in English.

About Us

Are you interested in joining one of Europe's leading PayTech companies?

Do you want to be part of a diverse team, collaborating with colleagues from across Europe? If so, you're in the right place!

About The Team

We are the result of merging Nexi, Nets, and Sia, with a presence in over 25 countries. With over 80 years of experience, we offer plenty of opportunities for personal and professional growth

At Nexi, we learn, adapt, and think like entrepreneurs. Discover more about our culture by visiting this page: https://www.nexigroup.com/en/people/life-at-nexi/overview/?utm_source=career-platform&utm_medium=job-openings&utm_content=about-the-team

Title: Junior Business Intelligence Specialist
Description:
Bolton Home, Personal Care & Beauty

Sales

Milano, IT

Junior Business Intelligence Specialist

We are looking for a Junior Business Intelligence Specialist to join Trade Marketing function in our Beauty Business Unit based in Milan.

How you make the difference

In this role, you will support the transformation of data into meaningful insights to help the business understand performance and trends. You will assist in analyzing sales data, market information, and consumer behavior to identify patterns and improvement opportunities.

You will work closely with cross‑functional teams to help develop dashboards, reports, and basic analytical tools that provide visibility on business results. With guidance from senior colleagues, you will contribute to data collection, analysis, and reporting activities, supporting forecasting processes and day‑to‑day decision‑making.

How Your Agenda Looks Like


Support the monthly Sales & Operations Planning (S&OP) process by collecting data, preparing analyses, and contributing to review meetings.
Assist in the development and maintenance of KPI dashboards to monitor business performance; help analyze trends and support simple forecasting and scenario analyses in collaboration with Sales and Supply teams.
Contribute to improving forecast accuracy by supporting Sales, Trade & Marketing teams in validating sales and volume projections.
Help ensure alignment between demand plans and commercial targets by supporting product launch planning and monitoring base business roll‑out.
Support the integration of S&OP activities with financial planning processes (budget and rolling forecast) by preparing data and analyses that link operational plans to financial results, in collaboration with the Finance team.


Your Team

You will join the Italy Marketing & Trade Marketing team, collaborating closely with Sales and Finance

What Makes You a Great Fit


Education: Master’s degree in Economics or related fields
Work Experience: 1-2 years of experience in Trade Marketing, F&A, Sales or related field
Technical Skills: proficiency in the Office suite, especially Excel and good analytical skills
Strong analytical and strategic thinking, with a proactive, autonomous approach and solid coordination and collaboration skills.
Local Language: Excellent knowledge of italian and english


What’s In For You


Work Location: Milan, Via Pirelli
Contract Type: Temporary local contract
Working Hours: Full-time
Remote Working: Standard business hours with remote working options available

Sales

Milano, IT

Junior Business Intelligence Specialist

We are looking for a Junior Business Intelligence Specialist to join Trade Marketing function in our Beauty Business Unit based in Milan.

How you make the difference

In this role, you will support the transformation of data into meaningful insights to help the business understand performance and trends. You will assist in analyzing sales data, market information, and consumer behavior to identify patterns and improvement opportunities.

You will work closely with cross‑functional teams to help develop dashboards, reports, and basic analytical tools that provide visibility on business results. With guidance from senior colleagues, you will contribute to data collection, analysis, and reporting activities, supporting forecasting processes and day‑to‑day decision‑making.

How Your Agenda Looks Like

  • Support the monthly Sales & Operations Planning (S&OP) process by collecting data, preparing analyses, and contributing to review meetings.
  • Assist in the development and maintenance of KPI dashboards to monitor business performance; help analyze trends and support simple forecasting and scenario analyses in collaboration with Sales and Supply teams.
  • Contribute to improving forecast accuracy by supporting Sales, Trade & Marketing teams in validating sales and volume projections.
  • Help ensure alignment between demand plans and commercial targets by supporting product launch planning and monitoring base business roll‑out.
  • Support the integration of S&OP activities with financial planning processes (budget and rolling forecast) by preparing data and analyses that link operational plans to financial results, in collaboration with the Finance team.

Your Team

You will join the Italy Marketing & Trade Marketing team, collaborating closely with Sales and Finance

What Makes You a Great Fit

  • Education: Master’s degree in Economics or related fields
  • Work Experience: 1-2 years of experience in Trade Marketing, F&A, Sales or related field

  • Strong analytical and strategic thinking, with a proactive, autonomous approach and solid coordination and collaboration skills.
  • Local Language: Excellent knowledge of italian and english

What’s In For You

Title: Data Analyst - SQL
Description:
Descrizione azienda

MC Engineering è una dinamica società di consulenza informatica, con sedi a Torino, Milano, Roma e Bari. Lavoriamo a stretto contatto con realtà innovative nel mondo ICT, portando avanti progetti che spaziano tra vari settori.

Per noi, le persone sono al centro di tutto: puntiamo su un continuo aggiornamento, sulla crescita costante e sull’eccellenza, per affrontare insieme sfide innovative e fare la differenza nel nostro settore.

Posizione

Cosa farai con noi?

Come Data Analyst - SQL avrai l’opportunità di lavorare su un progetto in ambito Finance, occupandoti della mappatura di stored procedure, KPI e logiche di calcolo non documentate, con l’obiettivo di migliorare la comprensibilità e l’operatività quotidiana di team tecnici e funzionali. Contribuirai inoltre alle attività di Data Governance, supportando la strutturazione di metadati, business glossary e la tracciabilità delle informazioni. Opererai in un contesto collaborativo e strutturato, orientato alla qualità e all’innovazione, dove il tuo contributo sarà valorizzato attraverso un percorso di crescita professionale.

Requisiti

Competenze richieste


Laurea o diploma tecnico
Esperienza di almeno 2+ anni nel ruolo di Data Analyst o Technical Analyst
Buona conoscenza di SQL e strumenti di interrogazione dati (SQL engine)
Esperienza intermedia con strumenti di reporting Microsoft (Power BI, SSRS o equivalenti)
Familiarità con Data Lineage e tracciamento dei flussi dati
Conoscenza di Business Glossary e definizione di metadati funzionali
Attitudine al lavoro in contesti con forte disallineamento tra dati e documentazione


Lingua inglese

Conoscenza intermedia della lingua inglese (livello B1)

Modalità e/o Sede di Lavoro

Modalità ibrida con 2-3 giorni a settimana presso una delle sedi di Milano o Roma

Completano il profilo


Un’elevata attitudine al lavoro in team
Doti di precisione e flessibilità mentale
Capacità proattiva nella gestione delle problematiche professionali


Altre informazioni

Cosa offriamo

Inserimento in organico con un inquadramento commisurato all’esperienza professionale pregressa e una retribuzione in linea con le reali capacità del candidato. Se sei una persona appassionata di tecnologia, desiderosa di crescere in un ambiente che promuove l’eccellenza nel settore Finance, questa è l’occasione che fa per te! Unisciti a MC Engineering: dove le idee diventano innovazione!

Il presente annuncio è rivolto ad entrambi i sessi, ai sensi delle leggi 903/77 e 125/91, e a persone di tutte le età e tutte le nazionalità, ai sensi dei DLgs 215/03 e 216/03.

Title: Jr. Data Strategist - Internship
Description:
Cheil Italy, the data-driven creativity Agency under the Samsung Group, is looking for a curious, analytical and AI-oriented Data Strategist Intern to join our Performance & Strategy Team.




This internship is designed for students eager to gain hands-on experience in digital performance analysis, data interpretation and strategic planning within a dynamic and data-driven agency environment.




Your Role




You will support the team in the strategy, development and monitoring of performance-driven communication activities for our main clients.




You will contribute to:

• Supporting the execution and monitoring of multi-channel activities, tracking performance against objectives and key metrics.

• Analysing data to extract insights and support strategic recommendations.

• Assisting in the preparation of reports and presentations for internal and external stakeholders.

• Contributing to the creation of dashboards, graphs and data visualizations.

• Supporting the setup and optimization of tracking and reporting processes.




Requirements




• Academic background in Marketing, Economics, Data Science, Statistics, Management Engineering or related fields.




• Strong interest in AI and data innovation: curiosity about AI applications in performance analysis, automation, predictive models or data-driven strategy, with a proactive attitude in exploring AI tools.

• Strong analytical mindset and attention to detail.

• Good knowledge of Excel.

• Familiarity (academic or personal) with Data Visualization tools such as Power BI, Tableau or similar.

• Basic understanding of digital performance metrics and KPIs.

• Good level of English.

• Team player mindset, curiosity and willingness to learn in a fast-paced environment.




Place of work: Milan




About Cheil




Cheil Italia Srl is a subsidiary of Cheil Worldwide Inc.




We redefine the way brands connect and engage with consumers.




Data is our foundation, technology is our building block. We drive our clients to address change creating 360° communication projects mixing advertising, digital marketing, retail and experiential marketing.




Our ethical approach




We are a certified Agency with the following certifications:




• Certified Quality System – ISO9001




• Certified Anti-Bribery – ISO37001




• Certified Carbon Footprint - ISO14064




• Certified Gender-Equality – UNI 125:2022




• Occupational Health & Safety – ISO45001




We are an equal opportunity employer




All aspects of employment including the decision to hire, promote, discipline, or discharge, will be based on merit, competence, performance, and business needs. We do not discriminate based on race, color, religion, age, physical or mental disability, medical condition, pregnancy, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Leroy Merlin, brand internazionale del gruppo Adeo, leader nel settore retail home improvement, bricolage e fai-da-te, è presente nelle principali città italiane con una missione chiara: aiutare le persone a realizzare i loro progetti di casa e di vita, offrendo soluzioni innovative e ad alto impatto positivo.

🎯 Chi cerchiamo

Stiamo cercando un/una Senior Data Analyst da inserire nel team Analytics della direzione Finance & Performance. Con un focus prevalente sull’area Marketing & CRM, il ruolo agisce come partner strategico del business al fine di contribuire all’ottimizzazione e allo sviluppo della strategia di customer retention di Leroy Merlin Italia sui diversi canali.

🚀 Responsabilità del ruolo

Lavorare con i propri stakeholder per comprenderne problemi, obiettivi e priorità, detenendo ownership su identificazione e implementazione delle soluzioni.
Diventare domain expert dell’area CRM e Loyalty, contribuendo allo sviluppo della data platform aziendale insieme al team Data Engineering, con l’obiettivo di rimuovere le soluzioni legacy e evitare la generazione di debito tecnico.
Sviluppare dashboard, analisi e prodotti data, con grande attenzione alla trasmissibilità degli stessi in un’ottica di industrializzazione degli asset.
Gestire in autonomia backlog e priorità, concentrandosi su attività a maggior valore aggiunto.
Comunicare i risultati del proprio lavoro in modo chiaro e accessibile, favorendone la comprensione e garantendo - quando possibile - una stima dell'impatto delle decisioni prese grazie al proprio contributo.
Diffondere competenze e best practice analitiche, diventando riferimento per Business Partner e Performance Leader.

✅ Qualifiche e competenze

5 o più anni di esperienza in ruolo analogo.
Padronanza di SQL e buona familiarità con Python.
Attitudine problem-first e comprovata esperienza nella capacità di definire il successo di iniziative basandosi su outcome (risultati finali / impatto) vs output (deliverable di progetto).
Esperienza con metodologie statistiche e modelli predittivi, in particolare nel ramo disciplinare del marketing e dell’analisi di incrementalità (uplift modeling, causal inference, counterfactual analysis).
Familiarità con i concetti di attribuzione digitale e le dinamiche di customer churn e retention.
Conoscenza di strumenti di analisi e visualizzazione dati (es. Power BI, Tableau, Looker)
Conoscenza della lingua inglese fluente

🎁 Cosa offriamo

🔹Percorsi di crescita locali ed internazionali e sviluppo continuo attraverso HUB formativo online e training on the job.

🔹Sistema premiante che valorizza le performance individuali e di squadra.

🔹Azionariato diffuso Gruppo Adeo.

🔹Portale convenzioni, scontistiche collaboratori.

🔹Contratto a tempo indeterminato.

🔹Mensa aziendale: perché anche il benessere passa dal buon cibo!

🔹Ambiente dinamico e flessibile: dopo la fase di inserimento, potrai accedere alla nostra policy di smart working ibrido, alternando giornate in sede e da remoto.

Accogliamo con favore le candidature di tutti i membri della società e crediamo che l'impiego di una forza lavoro diversificata sia fondamentale per il nostro successo.

🎯Vuoi entrare a far parte della squadra Leroy Merlin?

👉 Inviaci la tua candidatura!

Title: Data Analyst Junior
Description:
Everience è un gruppo internazionale di consulenza che offre servizi digitali potenziati dall’intelligenza artificiale e pone le persone al centro della rivoluzione dell’AI. Presente in Europa, Africa, Asia e America, Everience offre ai suoi 4.000 collaboratori un ambiente stimolante e sfidante in cui trasformare e sviluppare le proprie competenze, esplorando nuovi ruoli basati sull’AI e costruendo la propria occupabilità futura. Attraverso la sua Symbiotic Academy, il gruppo offre un hub unico per formazione, applicazione pratica e condivisione, dove ognuno può sperimentare, apprendere e progredire nei campi dell’intelligenza artificiale e dei dati.

In linea con la sua missione di orchestrare la relazione simbiotica tra esseri umani e intelligenza artificiale sul posto di lavoro, Everience fa del dipendente aumentato il motore di un’“età simbiotica”, in cui l’AI valorizza i talenti e apre nuove opportunità di carriera.







Siamo alla ricerca di un Data Analyst Junior per attività presso nostro cliente diretto. In questo ruolo, ti occuperai di trasformare i dati in informazioni utili che guidino le decisioni aziendali e sostengano la crescita dell'organizzazione. Lavorerai in collaborazione con team interfunzionali per identificare le esigenze in materia di dati, sviluppare analisi complete e fornire report chiari che consentano agli stakeholder di prendere decisioni informate.




Qualifiche




Laurea triennale in informatica, scienza dei dati, matematica, statistica o in un campo affine
2-3 anni di esperienza professionale come analista di dati
Ottima padronanza di SQL per l'estrazione, la trasformazione e l'analisi dei dati
Esperienza pratica con strumenti di BI (Power BI)
Ottime competenze nella visualizzazione dei dati e nello sviluppo di dashboard
Solida comprensione delle metodologie di analisi dei dati e dei concetti statistici
Capacità di lavorare in modo autonomo e di prendere l'iniziativa nei progetti
Esperienza nella collaborazione con gli stakeholder aziendali per tradurre i requisiti in soluzioni analitiche
Buona conoscenza della lingua inglese (livello B2)




Ulteriori informazioni




L'attività è in full-time presso la sede del cliente sita a Sovico (MB).

E' prevista modalità di lavoro in smart working (1 giorno alla settimana)

Valutiamo assunzione con contratto a tempo indeterminato (CCNL Commercio).




Tutti i nostri annunci sono rivolti ad entrambi i sessi, ai sensi delle leggi 903/77 e 125/91, e a persone di tutte le età e tutte le nazionalità, ai sensi dei decreti legislativi 215/03 e 216/03.

Title: Reporting & Data Automation Specialist
Description:
MET Group is an integrated European energy company, headquartered in Switzerland, with activities in natural gas and power, focused on multi-commodity wholesale, trading and sales, as well as energy infrastructure and industrial assets.



MET Group is present in 30 national gas markets and 39 international trading hubs. The Group has a significant end-consumer presence in Croatia, Italy, Hungary, Romania, Slovakia, and Spain.



MET Group has 1000+ permanent staff. The company is owned 90% by MET employees and 10% by Keppel Infrastructure, a wholly owned subsidiary of Keppel Corporation*.



MET Energia Italia is the Italian subsidiary or MET Group, with activities in natural gas, power and renewables, focused on multi-commodity trading and sales, as well as renewables and Energy Efficiency Solutions. After its start up in 2017, MET Energia Italia has now reached a position among the top 20 energy players in the country.



MET Energia Italia’s mission is to become a leading market player by implementing innovation in the Italian energy market. We are a boutique of energy professionals who, thanks to our expertise and hard work, have been able to establish a competitive and highly optimized business in a few years. We are determined to take our success story even further, so we strive to attract highly talented people that are ready to give their best every day and that are eager to continuously develop their skills by taking on big challenges.





Job Description



As we continue to evolve our client portfolio and expand, MET Energia Italia is now looking for a talented Reporting & Data Automation Specialist, who will be contributing to the growth of the company in the Italian market. In this role, you will be responsible for the evolution of the company’s reporting systems, with the goal of increasing their quality, reliability, and level of automation, while reducing manual activities and improving the integration of data coming from multiple sources. You will combine strong Excel skills, IT capabilities, and process vision, acting as a bridge between Finance, Controlling, Credit Management, and technical/IT infrastructures.



Step by step you will take on several activities bringing your invaluable contribution to our daily operations:



Automate data collection and consolidation processes (e.g., from ERP, management systems, external sources)
Design, develop, and maintain structured and automated reporting systems based on databases and integrated data flows (data extraction, normalization, aggregation), transforming manual reports into advanced, dynamic, scalable models for: economic–financial analyses, performance KPIs, margin analysis and cost controlling
Improve data quality (accuracy, consistency, traceability, reconciliations)
Develop dashboards and periodic reports for Management and for the operational functions of the AFC and Credit Management areas
Support internal and external audits
Collaborate with IT and external providers to integrate systems and optimize data sources, while maintaining finance ownership of the figures
Supporting the launch of new ideas and projects to grow the business
Be active and ready to take on new challenges in other areas of MET should this become your desire


Qualifications


BSc or MSc in Statistics or Economics, with a strong academic track record
5 years of relevant experience in similar roles
Familiarity with financial reporting and management control (budgeting, forecasting, and variance analysis)
Ability to read and interpret the Income Statement and Balance Sheet
Data integration from ERP systems (e.g., Microsoft Navision, Business Central) and management systems
Hands-on experience with BI tools (e.g., Power BI) and CRM/Salesforce platforms, will be considered a plus
Sharp analytical and data‑driven mindset with a strong financial and business partnering acumen
Strong attention to accuracy, combined with autonomy, reliability, and a high sense of confidentiality
Proven capacity to work effectively under recurring deadlines in a fast‑paced environment
Fluent in Italian with a strong command of English
Proficient in MS Office, with advanced Excel skills for analysis and reporting


Additional Information



In this role, you will have the opportunity to make a big impact to the overall results of the company. You will be part of a dynamic, innovative growth company which embraces new ideas and encourages everyone to contribute to our strategy.



We have an open, informal culture and we trust our people to get the job done - so we will also give you plenty of autonomy.



You will be working in an international environment which creates opportunities to share knowledge and work together with colleagues from other MET subsidiaries, mainly from the Swiss office.

As we continue to evolve our client portfolio and expand, MET Energia Italia is now looking for a talented Reporting & Data Automation Specialist, who will be contributing to the growth of the company in the Italian market. You will combine strong Excel skills, IT capabilities, and process vision, acting as a bridge between Finance, Controlling, Credit Management, and technical/IT infrastructures.

Step by step you will take on several activities bringing your invaluable contribution to our daily operations:

  • Automate data collection and consolidation processes (e.g., from ERP, management systems, external sources)
  • Design, develop, and maintain structured and automated reporting systems based on databases and integrated data flows (data extraction, normalization, aggregation), transforming manual reports into advanced, dynamic, scalable models for: economic–financial analyses, performance KPIs, margin analysis and cost controlling
  • Improve data quality (accuracy, consistency, traceability, reconciliations)
  • Develop dashboards and periodic reports for Management and for the operational functions of the AFC and Credit Management areas
  • Support internal and external audits
  • Collaborate with IT and external providers to integrate systems and optimize data sources, while maintaining finance ownership of the figures

  • Familiarity with financial reporting and management control (budgeting, forecasting, and variance analysis)

  • Data integration from ERP systems (e.g., Microsoft Navision, Business Central) and management systems

  • Sharp analytical and data‑driven mindset with a strong financial and business partnering acumen
  • Strong attention to accuracy, combined with autonomy, reliability, and a high sense of confidentiality
  • Proven capacity to work effectively under recurring deadlines in a fast‑paced environment

Additional Information

You will be working in an international environment which creates opportunities to share knowledge and work together with colleagues from other MET subsidiaries, mainly from the Swiss office.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Job description:



Founded in 1913 in Italy, the Prada Group was built on a tradition of excellence and with a vision of innovation. The Group, a world leader in the luxury sector, operates in more than 45 countries with the PRADA, Miu Miu, Versace, Church’s, Car Shoe and Luna Rossa brands, and has employees of over 100 nationalities.

 

 

 

JOB PURPOSE

 

We are looking for a Data Engineer to join our global Cloud Data Platform team, based in Milan. The ideal candidate will play a key role in managing data operations, with main activities ranging from analysis, modeling and development, with a strong focus on sales, customer, and marketing data.

 

 

RESPONSIBILITIES

 

Gain a deep understanding of business and technical needs to support model evolution and drive digital transformation. A particular focus will be on customer intelligence data and sales KPIs.
Design, maintain, and enhance key datasets to ensure they are high-quality, certified, and easily accessible/integrable by enterprise users, components, and systems.
Analyze and develop data operations and pipelines in line with enterprise guidelines and best practices (e.g., data quality processes, governance, and deep catalog/glossary curation).
Work collaboratively with business stakeholders and end users to build scalable and agile solutions.
Develop, deploy, and manage highly efficient and automated data pipelines using cloud-based technologies and platform capabilities (e.g., Azure, Databricks, Unity Catalog, Git based CI/CD).
Continuously adapt to evolving requirements by maintaining and improving existing data pipelines, integrating new features and change requests using an agile approach.

 

 

KNOWLEDGE AND SKILLS

 

Bachelor or Master's degree in Software Engineering, Computer Science, Mathematics or related STEM discipline

3+ years experience in intensive data ops. in the context of Big Data and cloud infrastructures / platforms

Strong experience in analytics in the context of retail industry, with a particular focus on modeling and leveraging sales and customer KPIs.

Strong background in Big Data approaches and DBMS/Data Warehouse modeling, optimization, and management. Deep knowledge of SQL and Spark-related programming languages is a must.

Extensive expertise in data preparation, integration, modeling, and governance processes.

Proven experience in designing and managing end-to-end production ready solutions.

Experience in developing microservices and APIs solutions (e.g., REST, OAuth).

Proficiency in configuring and utilizing cloud infrastructures and platforms, with a strong preference for Microsoft Azure services.

Familiarity with stream-processing systems and lambda architectures, Delta Live Tables,  Azure Event Hubs, Spark Streaming, etc.

Fluent in Italian and in English written and spoken

A good knowledge of retail and/or customer related processes and back end operational systems will be considered a plus

 

 

 

Joining our Company means working in a creative and international environment, with teams of people motivated by curiosity and the quest for excellence. The engine of our success is the importance and value that we place on the talent and passion of our people leading to their own professional growth.
Prada Group promotes an inclusive work environment, ensuring equal opportunities for all candidates, regardless of gender, ethnicity, sexual orientation, disability, or other personal characteristics. We believe that diversity is a value and we are committed to build a fair and respectful workplace for all.

Title: Elite - Business Data Analyst
Description:
Euronext is the leading pan-European market infrastructure, connecting European economies to global capital markets, to accelerate innovation and sustainable growth. It operates regulated exchanges in Belgium, France, Ireland, Italy, the Netherlands, Norway and Portugal.

The Group provides a multi-asset clearing house through Euronext Clearing, and custody and settlement services through Euronext Securities central securities depositories in Denmark, Italy, Norway and Portugal. Euronext also leverages its expertise in running markets by providing technology and managed services to third parties. In addition to its main regulated market, it also operates a number of junior markets, simplifying access to listing for SMEs.

About ELITE

ELITE is the Euronext ecosystem to support private companies by connecting them to skills, network and capital to drive their sustainable growth in the long-term, allowing them to access private and public capital markets.

The ELITE Offer Is Built Around Three Pillars

Access to skillset and expertise, via a proprietary training program covering a range of topics, from growth strategy to innovation and internationalization, from risk management to corporate governance to funding options. Access to an international network: ELITE members benefit from exposure to leading peers and advisors, as well as media coverage and access to exclusive events Access to growth capital, via traditional or alternative funding solutions, acting as a bridge from private to public.

Since ELITE’s launch, more than 2.300 companies have been part of the ELITE network, which today counts 1.500+ companies with an aggregate revenue of around €188 billion, representing 39 sectors and employing 652.000 people. ELITE has been a successful story, providing concrete support to many companies to accelerate their growth: €947 million raised with direct support from ELITE for over 260 companies leveraging also on the Basket Bond model, 76 companies have been listed raising €3.8 billion, 187 companies issued 269 bonds raising €3,55 billion, and 528 companies managed over 1.750 M&A deals.

ELITE – Business Data Analyst

The resource - within the Strategy and Business Development division - will support data-driven activities and strategic initiatives by gathering, organizing and analysing information from complex datasets to extract actionable insights in order to enhance business performance and decision-making, both for internal scope (i.e. analysis, reporting, new members onboarding, …) and for business development insights (i.e. clients' needs mapping, data strategy evolution, data-driven upselling/cross selling opportunities).

The Business Data Analyst will work in coordination with the other ELITE's business divisions including product, sales and marketing to continuously enhance and foster a data driven approach across all ELITE’s functions.

Activities will involve data operations and manipulation tasks including cleaning and integration. The resource will use tools such as Excel and query languages to transform and enrich data through data mapping techniques, and tools such as Power BI to analyse and visualize data and insights.

Workplace: Milan, Palazzo Mezzanotte

Key Responsibilities


Data Management: Analysis, quality assurance, integration of data collected from the community to produce solid data sets usable for internal and external analysis, visualization and future machine learning operations. Valorization and integration of unstructured data, classification and extraction of relevant information from different data sources. Ensure data quality and maintenance of data assets (internal DB) by implementing best practices in data management and governance.
Data Analysis & Insights: Analyze datasets to identify trends, patterns, and opportunities for business improvement. Provide actionable insights based on data in order to monitor and enhance business performance.
Reporting & Visualization: Create and maintain dashboards, reports and visualizations (Excel, Power BI, …) to communicate key metrics and insights effectively.
Support on Product & Market development:
Build specific, sector-oriented knowledge for the different products/solutions to be developed or tested with current and prospect clients Europe-wide
Help to identify market needs and opportunities through data analytics and provide actionable potential offer/product and sales activities (outbound)
Collaborate with cross-functional teams (sales, marketing, product) to support the development of new products/solutions and maintenance of existing products/solutions in line with client requirements and data mapping
Contribution on revenue management: enhance data management and strategy to continuously improve and maintain revenues (including on a recurring basis) with existing and new clients.
Client success: support the team to ensure that the service/product is successfully delivered to the clients. Identify upselling/cross-selling opportunities.

Candidate Profile/ Key Skills


Education: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Data Science, Statistics, Computer Science, Mathematics, Management Engineering, Economics, or a related field.
Proven experience (at least 2y) in data management, analytics and insight generation. Experience in a role to support business development, strategy, innovation or sales is a plus.
Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills with the ability to analyze complex data and provide actionable insights.
Proficiency in data analysis tools and languages, including Excel Power Queries, R, SQL and Python) and data visualization tools like Power BI.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to clearly and concisely present complex information to non-analytical stakeholders.
Ability to leverage internal and external relationships, drive forward projects and provide clear business input into functional requirements.
Proactive attitude, precision and attention to detail, ability to adapt in a dynamic and cross-functional environment.
Collaborative, open, pragmatic, team-worker, customer oriented and data driven approach to work.
Fluency in English


We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate against individuals on the basis of race, gender, age, citizenship, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, disability, or any other legally protected factor. We value the unique talents of all our people, who come from diverse backgrounds with different personal experiences and points of view and we are committed to providing an environment of mutual respect.

Additional Information

This job description is only describing the main activities within a certain role and is not exhaustive. It does not prevent to add more tasks, projects.

Title: Clinical Data Information JR Specialist
HTML:
Il Gruppo Humanitas, riferimento di eccellenza per la salute, la prevenzione e la diagnosi, è presente in Italia con 11 ospedali ad alta specializzazione, 25 Medical Care – la nostra rete di centri medici e punti prelievo, un Centro di Ricerca e Humanitas University, ateneo internazionale dedicato alle life sciences.

Ogni anno, oltre 1.300.000 pazienti scelgono di affidarsi agli specialisti delle nostre strutture presenti in Lombardia, Piemonte e Sicilia.

L’attenzione alle persone è il cuore di ogni scelta, perché la qualità di Humanitas nasce da chi ne fa parte. Lavorare con noi significa contribuire a un futuro in cui cura, benessere e professionalità crescono insieme.

Per l’Ospedale San Pio X, siamo alla ricerca di una risorsa da inserire all'interno dell'area Flussi Informativi Sanitari.


La persona, inserita in un team snello e dinamico, si occuperà principalmente delle seguenti attività:

  • supporto alla gestione della catalogazione delle cartelle cliniche tramite il fornitore esterno;
  • supporto alla gestione del flusso in entrata delle cartelle cliniche, attraverso la registrazione su apposito database;
  • controllo della sezione amministrativa delle SDO e relative correzioni;
  • verifica degli errori formali di natura amministrativa tramite l’utilizzo dell’applicativo dedicato;
  • gestione degli esiti dei flussi SDO attraverso SMAF e correzione di eventuali anomalie riscontrate;
  • gestione delle richieste di cartelle cliniche pervenute telefonicamente o tramite e-mail;
  • supporto alla consultazione delle cartelle cliniche da parte di medici interni ed enti esterni, su specifica richiesta;
  • gestione della spedizione delle copie conformi delle cartelle cliniche agli aventi diritto;
  • rilascio delle copie conformi delle cartelle cliniche e consegna agli uffici interni competenti;
  • supporto alle attività dell’ufficio e del responsabile di riferimento, incluse attività di reportistica e invio dei flussi agli enti regionali competenti.


Siamo alla ricerca di una persona appassionata e motivata, con un solido background accademico in Statistica, Ingegneria Gestionale o Economia con indirizzo sanitario, per entrare a far parte del nostro team. La persona che cerchiamo ha le seguenti caratteristiche:


  • curiosità e interesse per il mondo sanitario e giuridico;
  • precisione e attenzione ai dettagli nelle attività quotidiane;
  • eccellenti capacità di problem solving e gestione dello stress in situazioni complesse;
  • flessibilità nel rispondere alle dinamiche in continua evoluzione del settore;
  • ottime doti relazionali per interagire con una varietà di stakeholder interni ed esterni.


La selezione è aperta sia a candidati con prima esperienza lavorativa che con breve esperienza (1-2 anni) in ruoli analoghi, preferibilmente nel settore ospedaliero, nelle funzioni operations, IT, pianificazione e controllo in aziende complesse.


In Humanitas le persone sono il nostro valore e investiamo nel benessere di chi lavora con noi creando un ambiente che mette in luce professionalità, crescita e qualità della vita.

Per questo motivo offriamo:

  • un programma di benessere a 360°, con iniziative dedicate alla salute sia fisica che psicologica;
  • strumenti concreti per la conciliazione vita-lavoro, tra cui misure a sostegno della genitorialità e sostenibilità finanziaria;
  • percorsi di formazione per valorizzare talenti e competenze in un contesto dinamico e innovativo.


Sede di lavoro: Milano

L'offerta è rivolta a candidati nel rispetto del Dlgs 198/2006 e dei Dlgs 215/2003 e 216/2003.

Title: Fraud Data Analyst - Luxembourg
Description:
Please note that this opportunity requires relocation to our Luxembourg office.

About Us

Satispay began by rethinking the simple act of a payment to remove the friction from our daily routines. But we didn't stop there. Today, we are building a complete financial platform designed to empower people and concretely improve their lives.

By giving our 6 million users a clear, open path to pay, save, and invest, we are evolving into the definitive destination for every financial need.

What You'll Be Doing

As our Fraud Data Analyst, you'll be the person who secures the future of digital payments by architecting sophisticated real-time defense strategies.

Here's What Your Day-to-day Will Look Like


Architect Real-Time Strategies – You will be responsible for our real-time decisioning engine, designing and deploying logic that combines ML scores with velocity features to make millisecond decisions.
Precision Rule Development – Translate complex fraud patterns into high-performing rules, optimizing the balance between False Positive Rates (FPR) and Gross Fraud Loss.
Deconstruct Payment Fraud – Proactively hunt for emerging schemes (ATO, social engineering) and turn these insights into end-to-end mitigation strategies.
Optimize the Decisioning Stack – Partner with Product and Engineering to define analytical requirements for new features and third-party data signals.
Calibrate ML & Heuristics – Work with Data Science to operationalize machine learning models, determining how to best "wrap" model scores with heuristic rules.
Champion Frictionless UX – Analyze security measures to identify unnecessary hurdles, designing dynamic friction strategies for a seamless user experience.
Strategic Performance Reporting – Monitor rule efficacy and portfolio health, presenting data-driven recommendations to senior leadership to evolve our risk appetite.


Expect challenges, collaboration, and the freedom to bring your ideas to life in a fast-moving and supportive environment. Things change quickly here, so be ready to adapt, take initiative, and shape your role as we grow.

Who We're Looking For

We need a problem-solver who loves teamwork and gets things done. If you're curious and ready for real ownership, you'll fit in! Does this sound like you?


Experience – You have between 3 and 7 years of professional experience, specifically within the risk, payments, or credit sectors.

About Us

Satispay began by rethinking the simple act of a payment to remove the friction from our daily routines. But we didn't stop there. Today, we are building a complete financial platform designed to empower people and concretely improve their lives.

By giving our 6 million users a clear, open path to pay, save, and invest, we are evolving into the definitive destination for every financial need.

What You'll Be Doing

As our Fraud Data Analyst, you'll be the person who secures the future of digital payments by architecting sophisticated real-time defense strategies.

Here's What Your Day-to-day Will Look Like

  • Architect Real-Time Strategies – You will be responsible for our real-time decisioning engine, designing and deploying logic that combines ML scores with velocity features to make millisecond decisions.
  • Precision Rule Development – Translate complex fraud patterns into high-performing rules, optimizing the balance between False Positive Rates (FPR) and Gross Fraud Loss.
  • Deconstruct Payment Fraud – Proactively hunt for emerging schemes (ATO, social engineering) and turn these insights into end-to-end mitigation strategies.
  • Optimize the Decisioning Stack – Partner with Product and Engineering to define analytical requirements for new features and third-party data signals.
  • Calibrate ML & Heuristics – Work with Data Science to operationalize machine learning models, determining how to best "wrap" model scores with heuristic rules.
  • Champion Frictionless UX – Analyze security measures to identify unnecessary hurdles, designing dynamic friction strategies for a seamless user experience.
  • Strategic Performance Reporting – Monitor rule efficacy and portfolio health, presenting data-driven recommendations to senior leadership to evolve our risk appetite.

Expect challenges, collaboration, and the freedom to bring your ideas to life in a fast-moving and supportive environment. Things change quickly here, so be ready to adapt, take initiative, and shape your role as we grow.

Who We're Looking For

We need a problem-solver who loves teamwork and gets things done. If you're curious and ready for real ownership, you'll fit in! Does this sound like you?

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Who We Are

FIS is Italy’s leading company in the development and production of active pharmaceutical ingredients and intermediates for the global pharmaceutical industry.

With three manufacturing sites and more than 2,300 professionals, we have been committed for nearly 70 years to research, quality, and sustainability.

Our products help improve the lives of millions of people worldwide—a responsibility we embrace with pride and passion.

Join our team and become part of a company that grows through the dedication of the people who shape it every day.




The role

We are seeking a Data Analyst with strong experience in Power BI and a solid background in UI/UX design principles. The ideal candidate combines analytical rigor with a user-centered mindset, transforming complex data into intuitive, visually compelling dashboards and insights that support data-driven decision-making across the organization.

This role requires close collaboration with business stakeholders, product owners, and technical teams to ensure analytical solutions are both accurate and easy to use.




Key responsibilities

Data Analysis and Business Intelligence

Translate business requirements into analytical models, metrics, and KPIs.
Working closely with Data Engineers to validate data accuracy, consistency, and integrity across multiple sources.
Support ad-hoc analysis requests and strategic decision-making initiatives.




Power BI Development

Design, develop, and maintain interactive Power BI reports and dashboards.
Build efficient data models.
Apply best practices for security, including row-level security (RLS).
Manage publication and versioning of dashboards.




UI/UX and Data Visualization

Apply UI/UX principles to create intuitive, user-friendly dashboards.
Design visual layouts that improve data storytelling and usability.
Conduct usability reviews and iterate dashboards based on user feedback.
Ensure consistency in visual standards, branding, and design systems.
Balance visual appeal with analytical clarity and performance.




Collaboration and Stakeholder Engagement

Work closely with business users to understand needs and translate them into analytical solutions.
Collaborate with data engineers, developers, and designers on end‑to‑end data products.
Document dashboards, data models, and metrics for transparency and reuse.
Provide training or guidance to end users on dashboard usage and interpretation.




Requirements

Technical skills

At least 4–5 years of experience as a Data Analyst or in equivalent roles within structured organisations.
Strong hands-on experience with Power BI.
Solid knowledge of DAX, Power Query, and data modeling concepts.
Working knowledge of UI/UX principles, especially as applied to data visualization.
Ability to communicate complex data insights clearly to non-technical audiences.




Nice to have

Experience in regulated environments (pharmaceutical, life sciences, financial services).
Experience with design tools such as Figma, Adobe XD, or Sketch.
Familiarity with data visualization best practices and frameworks (e.g., storytelling with data).
Understanding of ETL processes and data pipelines.
Exposure to Agile or product-driven development environments.




Soft skills

Ability to work both independently and collaboratively within multidisciplinary teams
Problem-solving mindset with a strong focus on results and solution quality
Good communication and presentation skills, including with non-technical stakeholders
Intellectual curiosity and a continuous learning attitude towards emerging technologies and methodologies




What we offer

The opportunity to contribute to a high-impact digital transformation project.
Exposure to advanced Data & AI technologies in an international and stimulating environment.
A collaborative work environment with highly qualified, innovation-driven teams.

Title: Intern - Data Analyst
Description:
When a leadership team lacks a clear way to see its own data, small points of friction begin to accumulate. Decisions are delayed because evidence is buried in several different places, or a team finds itself looking at the same spreadsheet week after week without actually understanding what has changed. This role exists to solve that specific problem: to take the raw movement of a client's operations and turn it into a quiet, usable record that helps them breathe easier. We are looking for an Intern Data Analyst who enjoys the process of tidying up information and making it plain for others to see.

About Olive Tree Consulting Group
Olive Tree Consulting Group helps leadership teams handle the repetitive management issues that make daily work harder than it needs to be. We aren't interested in large scale transformations or branded corporate methodologies. Instead, we focus on the practical movement of decisions, responsibilities, and priorities within a team. Usually, we are brought in when a team finds themselves reopening the same decisions, struggling with unclear ownership, or feeling buried by meetings that try to do too much at once.
Our work is small, intentional, and grounded in reality. We help teams put in place clearer decision habits, cleaner handoffs, and a more usable written record of their work. The goal is always a quieter, more sustainable management pattern around the work the team already owns. We don't ask people to rename their ordinary tasks or adopt complex software systems. We simply look at how things currently work and suggest practical adjustments to make those outcomes more reliable and less personally taxing for the people involved. It is about making management feel easier to hold.

About the role
In this role, your primary mission is to support our consultants by organizing the data that flows through our client engagements. You will not be building complex proprietary algorithms. Instead, you will be looking at how a team records its activities and finding ways to make those records more accurate and accessible. You will provide the factual foundation that allows a leadership team to see their own habits clearly. This is a full time, permanent position for someone starting their career who values precision and enjoys working in a steady, methodical environment.

What you will do
Reviewing weekly project logs to ensure that data entries are consistent and categories are correctly applied.
Creating simple, clear visualizations that show the movement of tasks and priorities over time for our clients.
Cleaning and formatting spreadsheets so they are easy for non technical managers to read and use during their meetings.
Identifying gaps in how information is collected and suggesting practical ways to capture missing details without adding more work for the team.
Assisting in the preparation of monthly update reports by aggregating data from various internal sources.
Updating our internal tracking systems to ensure the rhythm of our own consulting work remains visible and organized.
Participating in team reviews to discuss what the data suggests about a client's current management patterns and habits.


What we are looking for
We need someone who finds satisfaction in order and clarity. You should be comfortable with spreadsheets and have a basic understanding of how to organize data sets so they tell a coherent story. High level technical mastery is less important than a careful eye for detail and the ability to notice when something in a record does not look quite right. We value clear, plain writing and the ability to explain technical findings in common language. You should be someone who prefers a quiet work day focused on making things more usable for others.

Helpful background
Experience using tools like Excel or Google Sheets for data organization and basic analysis.
An interest in organizational behavior or how teams communicate and make decisions.
A background in a field that requires careful attention to detail, such as research, library science, or administrative support.
Familiarity with simple data visualization tools or a willingness to learn how to present data cleanly.


How we work
Working at Olive Tree Consulting Group is a remote experience designed to be sustainable and low friction. We do not value long hours or a sense of constant urgency. Instead, we value the quality of our observations and the usefulness of our output. Communication happens primarily through written records and scheduled, purposeful calls. We believe that professional growth comes from doing real work well and having the time to think about the patterns we see. Our goal is to build a culture where your work contributes to a quieter, more effective outcome for our clients and a balanced, predictable life for our team.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Who Are We

Amaris Consulting is an independent technology consulting firm providing guidance and solutions to businesses. With more than 1,000 clients across the globe, we have been rolling out solutions in major projects since 2007 – this is made possible by an international team of 7,600 people spread across 5 continents and more than 60 countries. Our solutions focus on four different Business Lines: Information System & Digital, Telecom, Life Sciences and Engineering. We’re focused on building and nurturing a top talent community where all our team members can achieve their full potential.

At Amaris, we strive to provide our candidates with the best possible recruitment experience.

We like to get to know our candidates, challenge them, and be able to give them proper feedback as quickly as possible. Here's what our recruitment process looks like:

Brief Call: Our process typically begins with a brief virtual/phone conversation to get to know you! The objective? Learn about you, understand your motivations, and make sure we have the right job for you!

Interviews (the average number of interviews is 3 - the number may vary depending on the level of seniority required for the position). During the interviews, you will meet people from our team: your manager of course, but also other people related to your future role. We will talk in depth about you, your experience, and skills, but also about the position and what will be expected of you. Of course, you will also get to know Amaris Consulting: our culture, our roots, our teams, and your career opportunities!

Case study: Depending on the position, we may ask you to take a test. This could be a role play, a technical assessment, a problem-solving scenario, etc.

As every role is different, we adapt the process accordingly, always focusing on the best possible candidate experience. We look forward to meeting you![doc2]




Job Description

Join our team and start a new adventure in an international and dynamic environment, where you will be able to fulfil your career expectations in a fast-growing organization.

Being a consultant at Amaris Consulting means positioning yourself as a technical and functional expert in an industrial field, but above all, being an ambassador of our organization to clients, thanks to:

Your interpersonal skills and ability to listen, in order to build a strong relationship of trust with our clients and fully understand their challenges and issues.
Your curiosity and eagerness to learn, as we support over 1,000 clients from all sectors worldwide.

Your missions

Lead the integration of data from multiple sources into unified systems
Design, implement, and optimize data flows to ensure accuracy and reliability
Analyze large and complex datasets to identify trends and actionable insights
Develop and maintain automated solutions for data integration and reporting
Collaborate with business and technical teams to gather requirements and deliver robust data solutions
Ensure data quality and integrity throughout all processes
Provide technical support and training on data integration tools and best practices
Contribute to the continuous improvement of data management methodologies

Your profile

Experience in data analysis, data integration, and data flow management (minimum 5 years)
Proficiency with SQL and experience in ETL tools and data integration platforms
Strong knowledge of data modeling, data warehousing, and database management systems
Advanced skills in Python or R for data processing and automation
Experience with business intelligence tools (e.g., Power BI)
Academic background: Computer Science, Mathematics, Statistics, Information Technology, or related field
English and Italian fluent
You demonstrate strong analytical and problem-solving skills
You demonstrate excellent communication and interpersonal skills
You demonstrate the ability to work independently and collaboratively in a multicultural environment
You demonstrate attention to detail and a commitment to data quality

What we offer

An international community, bringing together 110+ different nationalities
An environment where trust has a central place: 70% of our key leaders started their careers at the first level of responsibilities
A robust training system with our internal Academy and 250+ available modules
A vibrant workplace that frequently gathers for internal events (afterworks, team buildings, etc.)
At Mantu, sustainability is part of everything we do. You’ll have the opportunity to turn your ideas into action and make a tangible impact. Every day, our teams bring our ESG commitments to life, from reducing our footprint to driving positive change within our communities. Through our WeCare Together program, you’ll be empowered to design and lead projects that create real social or environmental impact, with the company’s full support.
Remote policy: Hybrid work with presence in Milan office required once a week
Benefits: Local benefits package according to Milan office policy




Amaris Consulting is proud to be an equal-opportunity workplace. We are committed to promoting diversity within the workforce and creating an inclusive working environment. For this purpose, we welcome applications from all qualified candidates regardless of gender, sexual orientation, race, ethnicity, beliefs, age, marital status, disability, or other characteristics[doc2].

Title: Junior Business Intelligence Specialist
Description:
Bolton Home, Personal Care & Beauty

Sales

Milano, IT

Junior Business Intelligence Specialist

We are looking for a Junior Business Intelligence Specialist to join Trade Marketing function in our Beauty Business Unit based in Milan.

How you make the difference

In this role, you will support the transformation of data into meaningful insights to help the business understand performance and trends. You will assist in analyzing sales data, market information, and consumer behavior to identify patterns and improvement opportunities.

You will work closely with cross‑functional teams to help develop dashboards, reports, and basic analytical tools that provide visibility on business results. With guidance from senior colleagues, you will contribute to data collection, analysis, and reporting activities, supporting forecasting processes and day‑to‑day decision‑making.

How Your Agenda Looks Like


Support the monthly Sales & Operations Planning (S&OP) process by collecting data, preparing analyses, and contributing to review meetings.
Assist in the development and maintenance of KPI dashboards to monitor business performance; help analyze trends and support simple forecasting and scenario analyses in collaboration with Sales and Supply teams.
Contribute to improving forecast accuracy by supporting Sales, Trade & Marketing teams in validating sales and volume projections.
Help ensure alignment between demand plans and commercial targets by supporting product launch planning and monitoring base business roll‑out.
Support the integration of S&OP activities with financial planning processes (budget and rolling forecast) by preparing data and analyses that link operational plans to financial results, in collaboration with the Finance team.


Your Team

You will join the Italy Marketing & Trade Marketing team, collaborating closely with Sales and Finance

What Makes You a Great Fit


Education: Master’s degree in Economics or related fields
Work Experience: 1-2 years of experience in Trade Marketing, F&A, Sales or related field
Technical Skills: proficiency in the Office suite, especially Excel and good analytical skills
Strong analytical and strategic thinking, with a proactive, autonomous approach and solid coordination and collaboration skills.
Local Language: Excellent knowledge of italian and english


What’s In For You


Work Location: Milan, Via Pirelli
Contract Type: Temporary local contract
Working Hours: Full-time
Remote Working: Standard business hours with remote working options available


Curious About Us? Dive In:

About Bolton

Bolton is an Italian family-owned fast-moving consumer goods company with 10.500 people located across 60 offices, 17 production facilities, 10 R&D laboratories, and 14 fishing vessels worldwide. With a diverse portfolio of over 60 quality brands, Bolton has been enriching the lives of millions of people every day for more than 75 years, making their everyday more delicious, more enjoyable, useful, and beautiful.

About Beauty

Bolton Beauty is a Business Unit of Bolton that enhances the beauty of millions of people through much-loved brands such as Collistar and Cavailles. Making their everyday lives more glamorous, confident and beautiful. The Business Unit is committed to finding pure and simple cosmetic solutions in Italy and beyond.

How you make the difference

In this role, you will support the transformation of data into meaningful insights to help the business understand performance and trends. You will assist in analyzing sales data, market information, and consumer behavior to identify patterns and improvement opportunities.

You will work closely with cross‑functional teams to help develop dashboards, reports, and basic analytical tools that provide visibility on business results. With guidance from senior colleagues, you will contribute to data collection, analysis, and reporting activities, supporting forecasting processes and day‑to‑day decision‑making.

How Your Agenda Looks Like

  • Support the monthly Sales & Operations Planning (S&OP) process by collecting data, preparing analyses, and contributing to review meetings.
  • Assist in the development and maintenance of KPI dashboards to monitor business performance; help analyze trends and support simple forecasting and scenario analyses in collaboration with Sales and Supply teams.
  • Contribute to improving forecast accuracy by supporting Sales, Trade & Marketing teams in validating sales and volume projections.
  • Help ensure alignment between demand plans and commercial targets by supporting product launch planning and monitoring base business roll‑out.
  • Support the integration of S&OP activities with financial planning processes (budget and rolling forecast) by preparing data and analyses that link operational plans to financial results, in collaboration with the Finance team.

Your Team

You will join the Italy Marketing & Trade Marketing team, collaborating closely with Sales and Finance

What Makes You a Great Fit

  • Education: Master’s degree in Economics or related fields
  • Work Experience: 1-2 years of experience in Trade Marketing, F&A, Sales or related field
  • Technical Skills: proficiency in the Office suite, especially Excel and good analytical skills
  • Strong analytical and strategic thinking, with a proactive, autonomous approach and solid coordination and collaboration skills.
  • Local Language: Excellent knowledge of italian and english

What’s In For You

  • Work Location: Milan, Via Pirelli
  • Contract Type: Temporary local contract
  • Working Hours: Full-time
  • Remote Working: Standard business hours with remote working options available

Curious About Us? Dive In:

About Bolton

Bolton is an Italian family-owned fast-moving consumer goods company with 10.500 people located across 60 offices, 17 production facilities, 10 R&D laboratories, and 14 fishing vessels worldwide. With a diverse portfolio of over 60 quality brands, Bolton has been enriching the lives of millions of people every day for more than 75 years, making their everyday more delicious, more enjoyable, useful, and beautiful.

About Beauty

Bolton Beauty is a Business Unit of Bolton that enhances the beauty of millions of people through much-loved brands such as Collistar and Cavailles. Making their everyday lives more glamorous, confident and beautiful. The Business Unit is committed to finding pure and simple cosmetic solutions in Italy and beyond.

Grow Your Future with Us, Apply now

Title: Junior Data Analyst - Family Office
Description:
RUOLO

La risorsa opererà nell'ambito della divisione di Family Office, nella sede di Milano e verrà inserita all'interno del team di Middle Office & Data Management, supportando ed affiancando il team degli Analisti Finanziari dedicato al servizio di consulenza nell'Investment banking, nella selezione ed esecuzione di investimenti diretti ed investimenti in fondi Alternativi/Private equity, nell'assistenza in operazioni di finanza straordinaria (M&A, private debt, consulenza sui mercati dei capitali).

Le principali responsabilità saranno:

Gestire ed implementare la piattaforma informatica utilizzata per l'analisi dei portafogli dei clienti e per la produzione della reportistica
Contattare le controparti Banche/Brokers per richiedere l'attivazione del servizio di invio dei flussi informativi automatici e l'invio della rendicontazione bancaria al fine di eseguire la riconciliazione e la validazione delle posizioni in piattaforma
Eseguire gli upload manuali delle posizioni/strumenti Alternativi non pervenute tramite flusso di dati automatico, nella piattaforma informatica
Gestire in modo efficiente il flusso di comunicazioni all'interno del Team e con le controparti
Assistere il Team degli Analisti Finanziari per la risoluzione di eventuali anomalie presenti sulla piattaforma informatica, aprendo e gestendo i relativi tickets
Monitorare i valori dei portafogli al fine di intercettare eventuali scostamenti significativi e accertarne la coerenza

La risorsa, a contatto con analisti senior e manager esperti, acquisirà gradualmente il bagaglio di competenze tecniche e di soft skills necessario per svolgere con successo incarichi di crescente complessità e fornire un'assistenza professionale di alto livello ad una clientela sofisticata.




REQUISITI

Il candidato ideale dovrà essere proattivo e ben organizzato, con una ottima predisposizione al lavoro in team, una spiccata capacità di analisi dei documenti, sia quantitativa che qualitativa, unitamente ad un alto livello di precisione.

Si richiede, inoltre, una buona conoscenza della lingua inglese, una ottima conoscenza del pacchetto Office (in particolare Excel), una conoscenza di base nell'utilizzo di Bloomberg.

Eventuale precedente esperienza nell'ambito del Family Office/Private Banking è un plus.

Si richiede diploma o laurea triennale in ambito economico (Ragioneria, Economia, Ingegneria Gestionale o affini) o comunque in linea con i requisiti della candidatura.




BANOR SIM SPA

BANOR SIM è una boutique finanziaria indipendente costituita oltre 25 anni fa con l'idea di creare un polo di eccellenza nelle gestioni dei capitali e nella consulenza finanziaria su grandi patrimoni. Banor SIM Spa fa parte dell'universo BANOR insieme a BANOR Capital Ltd (Londra), BANOR SAM (Monaco) e BANOR Sicav (Lussemburgo), ha una squadra di oltre 150 professionisti e Asset Under Influence superiori a 11 miliardi di euro.

BANOR SIM ha relazioni internazionali consolidate da anni con i più prestigiosi Asset Manager, fondi di Private Equity, fondi di Private Debt, fondi di Venture Capital e primarie Società di Advisory.

Il team di Advisory del Family Office di Banor Sim, in costante crescita, conta oggi oltre 20 professionisti dedicati.




Si offre un contratto di stage della durata di 6 mesi, finalizzato a un successivo inserimento a tempo indeterminato in azienda, sulla base delle competenze dimostrate.




L’informativa al trattamento dei dati personali è raggiungibile al seguente link: https://www.banor.it/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/Informativa-dei-dati-personali-ai-candidati-3.pdf

Title: Junior Data Analyst
HTML:
Kìron Partner SpA, società di mediazione creditizia del Gruppo Tecnocasa, leader in Italia nella consulenza sul credito alla famiglia, è alla ricerca di un/una Junior Data Analyst da inserire all’interno del proprio team.



La posizione:

Entrerai a far parte di un team di 3 persone. Il nostro obiettivo è insegnarti tutto quello che serve per gestire ed elaborare i dati legati ai compensi della nostra rete commerciale. Verrai affiancato direttamente dal Responsabile di funzione che ti guiderà passo dopo passo nell'utilizzo dei nostri sistemi informatici.



Cosa imparerai a fare:

  • Verifica ed elaborazione dei flussi dati provenienti dai partner commerciali;
  • Inserimento dati sui gestionali aziendali di riferimento e produzione di reportistica;
  • Elaborazione degli schemi provvigionali e delle rendicontazioni compensi;
  • Gestione della comunicazione con la rete commerciale e con le funzioni interne;
  • Supporto al team nelle diverse attività e processi di settore.



Cosa cerchiamo:

  • Diploma di scuola superiore (preferibilmente in Ragioneria, Amministrazione, Geometra o simili);
  • Gradita esperienza in ambito aziende di servizi terziario;
  • Ottima dimestichezza con gli strumenti informatici, in particolare Excel;
  • Attitudine all’analisi e all’elaborazione dei dati;
  • Buone capacità di lavoro in team;
  • Precisione, autonomia e spirito di iniziativa.



Sede di lavoro: Rozzano (MI)

Orario: Full-Time



Inquadramento e pacchetto retributivo saranno definiti in relazione alle capacità e all’esperienza maturata dal candidato

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Job description:



Founded in 1913 in Italy, the Prada Group was built on a tradition of excellence and with a vision of innovation. The Group, a world leader in the luxury sector, operates in more than 45 countries with the PRADA, Miu Miu, Versace, Church’s, Car Shoe and Luna Rossa brands, and has employees of over 100 nationalities.

 

 

 

JOB PURPOSE

 

We are looking for a Data Engineer to join our global Cloud Data Platform team, based in Milan. The ideal candidate will play a key role in managing data operations, with main activities ranging from analysis, modeling and development, with a strong focus on sales, customer, and marketing data.

 

 

RESPONSIBILITIES

 

Gain a deep understanding of business and technical needs to support model evolution and drive digital transformation. A particular focus will be on customer intelligence data and sales KPIs.
Design, maintain, and enhance key datasets to ensure they are high-quality, certified, and easily accessible/integrable by enterprise users, components, and systems.
Analyze and develop data operations and pipelines in line with enterprise guidelines and best practices (e.g., data quality processes, governance, and deep catalog/glossary curation).
Work collaboratively with business stakeholders and end users to build scalable and agile solutions.
Develop, deploy, and manage highly efficient and automated data pipelines using cloud-based technologies and platform capabilities (e.g., Azure, Databricks, Unity Catalog, Git based CI/CD).
Continuously adapt to evolving requirements by maintaining and improving existing data pipelines, integrating new features and change requests using an agile approach.

 

 

KNOWLEDGE AND SKILLS

 

Bachelor or Master's degree in Software Engineering, Computer Science, Mathematics or related STEM discipline

3+ years experience in intensive data ops. in the context of Big Data and cloud infrastructures / platforms

Strong experience in analytics in the context of retail industry, with a particular focus on modeling and leveraging sales and customer KPIs.

Strong background in Big Data approaches and DBMS/Data Warehouse modeling, optimization, and management. Deep knowledge of SQL and Spark-related programming languages is a must.

Extensive expertise in data preparation, integration, modeling, and governance processes.

Proven experience in designing and managing end-to-end production ready solutions.

Experience in developing microservices and APIs solutions (e.g., REST, OAuth).

Proficiency in configuring and utilizing cloud infrastructures and platforms, with a strong preference for Microsoft Azure services.

Familiarity with stream-processing systems and lambda architectures, Delta Live Tables,  Azure Event Hubs, Spark Streaming, etc.

Fluent in Italian and in English written and spoken

A good knowledge of retail and/or customer related processes and back end operational systems will be considered a plus

 

 

 

Joining our Company means working in a creative and international environment, with teams of people motivated by curiosity and the quest for excellence. The engine of our success is the importance and value that we place on the talent and passion of our people leading to their own professional growth.
Prada Group promotes an inclusive work environment, ensuring equal opportunities for all candidates, regardless of gender, ethnicity, sexual orientation, disability, or other personal characteristics. We believe that diversity is a value and we are committed to build a fair and respectful workplace for all.

Title: Data Analyst (Finance)
Description:
About Revolut

People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 70+ million customers get more from their money every day.

As we continue our lightning-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution.

About The Role

Our Data Science team solves complex problems with smart, practical solutions. Data Analysts and Scientists work directly with product teams to uncover insights, guide decisions, and improve how customers experience Revolut. They do this by delivering smart, scalable solutions that move the business forward.

We’re looking for a Data Analyst who can dig into our complex databases, look for the root cause of a problem, and design their own solutions by writing code to implement them.

Up to shape what's next in finance? Let's get in touch.

What You'll Be Doing


Handling finance-related requests and providing ongoing data support
Managing the end-to-end delivery of data for MIS, regulatory reporting, and other operational reports
Performing data mapping and discovery to identify and document relevant data sources and business requirements
Executing ETL processes to ensure data is accurately extracted, transformed, and loaded for reporting and analysis
Designing key metrics to measure different aspects of the business, using data management tools to create more effective financial processes
Ensuring the integration and maintenance of new business into existing financial reporting systems, controls, and processes
Working with controllers to improve dashboards to track metrics, visualise insights, and identify business drivers
Developing and maintaining key relationships across the Finance team
Promoting data literacy across the company, organising and holding workshops


What You'll Need


4+ years of experience in a data analysis role
Great skills with Python and SQL
A solid background/education in a quantitative discipline
To be detail-oriented, quality-focused, and able to deliver under tight timeframes


Nice to have


A background in finance (ideally within a bank) and/or regulatory reporting experience
Experience preparing reports for external parties/authorities
Familiarity with Airflow, Trino, and Looker
Skills in other programming languages (Java, Scala, C++, etc.)


Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard-working team.

Important notice for candidates:

Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles.


Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third-party services or platforms for our recruitment.
Always double-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain.


We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process. If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately.

By submitting this application, I confirm that all the information given by me in this application for employment and any additional documents attached hereto are true to the best of my knowledge and that I have not wilfully suppressed any material fact. I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Revolut. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Revolut may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Revolut's Candidate Privacy Notice
