  • A solid background/education in a quantitative discipline
  • To be detail-oriented, quality-focused, and able to deliver under tight timeframes

Nice to have

  • A background in finance (ideally within a bank) and/or regulatory reporting experience
  • Experience preparing reports for external parties/authorities
  • Familiarity with Airflow, Trino, and Looker
  • Skills in other programming languages (Java, Scala, C++, etc.)

Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard-working team.

Important notice for candidates:

Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles.

  • Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third-party services or platforms for our recruitment.
  • Always double-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About Air Apps

At Air Apps, we believe in thinking bigger—and moving faster. We’re a family-founded company on a mission to create the world’s first AI-powered Personal & Entrepreneurial Resource Planner (PRP), and we need your passion and ambition to help us change how people plan, work, and live. Born in Lisbon, Portugal in 2018—and now with offices in both Lisbon and San Francisco—we’ve remained self-funded while reaching over 100 million downloads worldwide.

Our long-term focus drives us to challenge the status quo every day, pushing the boundaries of AI-driven solutions that truly make a difference. Here, you’ll be a creative force, shaping products that empower people across the globe.

Join us on this journey to redefine resource management—and change lives along the way.

The Role

As a Data Analyst at Air Apps, you will be responsible for analyzing and interpreting data to drive business insights and optimize product performance. You will create reports, dashboards, and performance metrics to help teams make informed decisions and improve user experience.

This role requires a data-driven mindset, strong analytical skills, and experience with visualization tools to turn complex data into actionable insights.


This is a fully onsite position, based at our office in Lisbon, where you will collaborate closely with cross-functional teams in person and contribute to a dynamic and fast-paced environment. We are open to support with relocation efforts.


Responsibilities


Analyze user behavior, product performance, and key business metrics to identify trends and opportunities.
Create and maintain interactive dashboards and reports using tools like Tableau, Looker, Power BI, or Google Data Studio.
Collect, clean, and structure large datasets to ensure accuracy and usability.
Define and track KPIs for marketing, product, and business performance.
Work with SQL, Python, or R to extract, analyze, and manipulate data.
Collaborate with product managers, engineers, and marketing teams to provide data-driven insights.
Conduct A/B testing and experiment analysis to improve product features and user engagement.
Identify bottlenecks and opportunities in user journeys and customer funnels.
Present findings in a clear and compelling way to stakeholders and leadership teams.
Stay up to date with industry trends, analytics best practices, and emerging data tools.


Requirements


Around 3+ years of experience in data analysis, business intelligence, or a related field.
Proficiency in SQL for data querying and manipulation.
Experience with data visualization tools (Tableau, Looker, Power BI, Google Data Studio).
Strong analytical skills with experience in Python or R for data analysis.
Knowledge of A/B testing methodologies and statistical analysis.
Experience with Google Analytics, Mixpanel, Amplitude, or similar user analytics tools.
Strong problem-solving skills and ability to communicate insights to non-technical stakeholders.
Experience working with large datasets and cloud-based data warehouses (BigQuery, Redshift, Snowflake).
Familiarity with ETL processes and data pipeline optimization is a plus.
Ability to work in a fast-paced, data-driven environment and manage multiple projects.


What benefits are we offering?


Apple hardware ecosystem for work.
Annual Bonus
Top-tier Health and Life Insurance for peace of mind.
Transportation Budget to support your commute needs.
Coverflex benefits package for meal allowances, well-being, and more.
Childcare support.
Air Conference - an opportunity to meet the team, collaborate, and grow together.
Pension Fund to support your long-term financial planning.
Urban Sports Club membership to keep you active.
Meals 100% free at the hub.


Diversity & Inclusion

At Air Apps, we are committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive, and equitable workplace. We enthusiastically welcome applicants from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We celebrate diversity in all its forms and believe that varied voices and experiences make us stronger.

Application Disclaimer

At Air Apps, we value transparency and integrity in our hiring process. Applicants must submit their own work without any AI-generated assistance. Any use of AI in application materials, assessments, or interviews will result in disqualification.

Compensation Range: €50K - €63K

Title: Data Analyst - Data Migration m/f/d - 873662/1
Description:
Für ein etabliertes Technologieumfeld suchen wir einen Data Analyst mit Fokus Data Migration, der aktiv daran mitwirkt, neue Kunden auf moderne Steuerlösungen zu migrieren – mit direktem Einfluss auf Qualität, Prozesse und Endergebnis.




Eckdaten auf einen Blick

Analyse komplexer Datenstrukturen und Datenbestände mittels SQL
Identifikation von Abhängigkeiten, Inkonsistenzen und Datenanomalien

Präsentation deiner Erkenntnisse gegenüber Stakeholdern
Aktive Unterstützung der Qualitätssicherung über den gesamten Prozess hinweg

Background in (Wirtschafts-)Informatik oder vergleichbar

Fundiertes Verständnis relationaler Datenbanken
Starkes analytisches Denken und Freude an komplexen Zusammenhängen
Fähigkeit, technische Themen verständlich zu kommunizieren
Sicheres Auftreten im Austausch mit verschiedenen Stakeholdern
Hands-on-Mentalität und Lösungsfokus

Title: Commercial Data Analyst
Description:
Für unseren Kunden, ein international ausgerichtetes Unternehmen im Pharma-/Healthcare-Umfeld, suchen wir per sofort Unterstützung durch eine/n erfahrene/n Commercial Data Analyst / Business Analyst.




In dieser Rolle unterstützen Sie das Management bei einem strategisch relevanten Analyseprojekt rund um Marktpotenziale, regionale Performance, Sales KPIs, Preis- und Volumenanalysen und Management Reporting. Ziel ist es, bestehende Markt-, Produkt-, Umsatz- und Sales-Daten strukturiert auszuwerten und daraus belastbare Entscheidungsgrundlagen für Geschäftssteuerung und Investitionsentscheidungen abzuleiten.




Ihre Aufgaben:

Analyse von Markt-, Preis-, Umsatz-, Produkt- und Sales-Daten
Erstellung und Weiterentwicklung von Management Reportings und Sales KPIs
Identifikation von Potenzialgebieten, regionalen Performance-Unterschieden und Investment-Logiken
Entwicklung von Modellen, Annahmen, Trendanalysen und Sensitivitätsanalysen
Unterstützung bei der Automatisierung von BI- und Reporting-Prozessen
Enge Zusammenarbeit mit Management und Sales




Ihr Profil:

Erfahrung als Data Analyst, Business Analyst, Commercial Analyst oder in einer vergleichbaren Rolle
Gute SQL-Kenntnisse
Erfahrung mit Business Analysis, Marktanalysen, KPIs und Management Reporting
Starkes analytisches Verständnis sowie Fähigkeit, auch mit unvollständigen Daten pragmatisch zu arbeiten
Erfahrung in Modellierungen, Szenario- und Sensitivitätsanalysen von Vorteil
Kommunikative, strukturierte und ergebnisorientierte Arbeitsweise
Deutschkenntnisse zwingend, Englisch für die Zusammenarbeit mit internationalen Stakeholdern erforderlich
Erfahrung im Pharma-, Healthcare-, MedTech- oder Life-Sciences-Umfeld ist ein Plus, aber kein Muss




Wenn Sie kurzfristig verfügbar sind und Freude daran haben, aus kommerziellen Daten konkrete Business Insights und Entscheidungsgrundlagen abzuleiten, freue ich mich auf Ihre Bewerbungsunterlagen.

Title: Data Visualizatio Analys
Description:
Job Title: Data Visualization Analyst

Location: Switzerland

Job Description:

We are looking for a talented Data Visualization Analyst to join our team in Switzerland. The ideal candidate will transform complex data into clear, actionable insights using advanced visualization techniques, helping stakeholders make informed business decisions.

Key Responsibilities:

Design, develop, and maintain interactive dashboards and visual reports using tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or Qlik.
Collect, clean, and analyze large datasets from multiple sources to identify trends, patterns, and insights.
Collaborate with business units to understand their data needs and translate them into visual solutions.
Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and integrity in all reports and visualizations.
Present findings to stakeholders in a clear and compelling manner, supporting strategic decisions.
Stay up-to-date with the latest visualization tools, trends, and best practices.

Qualifications & Skills:

Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Statistics, Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related field.
2–5 years of experience in data visualization, business intelligence, or analytics.
Proficiency with visualization tools such as Tableau, Power BI, Qlik, or equivalent.
Strong analytical skills and experience working with SQL, Excel, or other data analysis tools.
Knowledge of data warehousing concepts and experience with large datasets is a plus.
Strong communication skills in English; knowledge of French or German is an advantage.
Ability to translate complex data into actionable business insights.

What We Offer:

Competitive salary and benefits package.
Work in an international and collaborative environment.
Opportunities for professional growth and learning.

Title: Data Quality Analyst
Description:
Our client in Financial Services is looking for a Data Quality Analyst for a 6-months contract with possibility of extension or internalisation.




Responsibilities:

Identifying the underlying causes of data problems through review of trends, data movement, and source-to-target relationships
Linking data issues to operational processes and assessing how existing workflows generate inaccuracies or inconsistencies
Improving and restructuring processes to eliminate recurring issues, introducing controls, checks, and quality standards at the point of origin
Managing end-to-end resolution programmes that address both data correction and operational enhancement
Implementing governance and oversight models with clear accountability, performance metrics, and ongoing quality monitoring




Requirements:

More than five years of experience within data quality, data governance, or process improvement environment
Industry experience within financial services, gained either in-house or through a consulting environment
Strong investigative mindset with the ability to identify underlying causes and connect data issues to operational processes
Demonstrated track record of driving change initiatives from initial assessment through execution and delivery
Effective communicator who can work confidently with both technical specialists and senior business stakeholders




Interested or want to find out more? Drop us a message or apply below.

Title: Data Engineer (H/F)
Description:
Pour notre division Systèmes d’information, nous recherchons un Data Engineer (H/F) dont la mission principale sera la mise à disposition des données dans notre Data Platform hybride : cloud et on-premises, tout en garantissant qualité et sécurité. Le Data Engineer travaillera en étroite collaboration au sein du Centre de Data Excellence afin d’assurer la robustesse, l’évolutivité et la performance des environnements de données.

Responsabilités


Accompagner les équipes Data Science et Analytics dans l’industrialisation de leurs uses cases
Concevoir, déployer et maintenir des pipelines de données robustes
Développer, mettre en œuvre et automatiser des contrôles de validation de la qualité de données
Participer à la mise en place des solutions cloud (Snowflake, Azure, GCP) pour les environnements Data
Optimiser la consommation et les coûts des ressources
Favoriser l'extension de l'aspect As A Service de notre plateforme de données
Collaborer avec les équipes Infrastructures, Cloud et Sécurité pour garantir l’alignement avec les exigences et standards Rolex
Participer au support de niveau 3


Profil


Formation supérieure dans le domaine informatique ou ingénierie
Expérience significative en ingénierie des données
Connaissance approfondie des principes et méthodes de Data Quality
Maîtrise avancée des outils et technologies de gestion des données (Fivetran, Talend, Dataiku, Snowflake)
Expérience avec les principaux fournisseurs cloud (Azure, GCP) et outils DevOps (Terraform, Gitlab)
Maîtrise des concepts de sécurité de l'information
La connaissance de Tableau est un plus
Sens du service et de la communication, proactif, rigoureux, autonome, dynamique et doté d’un bon esprit d’équipe
Anglais professionnel


Nous vous offrons un environnement stimulant, d’excellentes conditions de travail, des solutions de flexibilisation du temps de travail et des prestations sociales de premier ordre.

Nous vous remercions d'adresser un dossier de candidature complet (CV, lettre de motivation, certificats et diplômes).

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Career Area

Technology, Digital and Data

Job Description

Your Work Shapes the World at Caterpillar Inc.

When you join Caterpillar, you're joining a global team who cares not just about the work we do – but also about each other. We are the makers, problem solvers, and future world builders who are creating stronger, more sustainable communities. We don't just talk about progress and innovation here – we make it happen, with our customers, where we work and live. Together, we are building a better world, so we can all enjoy living in it.

Job Summary

The Caterpillar Enterprise Strategy Group is seeking a Business Intelligence Analyst to pair quantitative techniques with business acumen, providing data-driven insight and analysis in support of our Rental & BCP Growth Plans with dealers. Primary roles/responsibilities for this position include, but are not limited to:


Utilizing multiple data sets throughout the enterprise and dealer network to identify data discrepancies, patterns, trends, and business insights.
Serve as a primary analytics partner to project teams, dealers, and cross‑functional stakeholders, ensuring analytical solutions directly support business decision‑making for Rental and BCP Growth projects.
Develop analytics tools and solutions in partnership with business stakeholders supporting dealer growth plans.
Translate complex analytical findings into clear, compelling narratives for enterprise leadership, dealer partners, and cross‑functional teams


What You Have


Intermediate to advanced Data Visualization (PowerBI) and Relational Database (SQL) skills
Exceptional communication skills with the ability to translate complex analytical insights into clear, actionable information for non-technical business audiences.
Extensive experience in Accuracy/Attention to Detail, Effective Communications, Business Data Analysis and Research Analysis
Working knowledge of Product/Solution Design Development, Technical Documentation and Requirements Analysis
Bachelor’s degree, preferably in business, marketing, economics, statistics, or a similar field with quantitative coursework.


Top Candidates Will Also Have


Prior experience in dealer-facing roles
Analytical programming experience (Microsoft Power Automate, Python etc)
Demonstrated Leadership attributes experience (Intellectual Curiosity, Good Business Skills/Judgement and Courage/Emotional Intelligence)


Additional Information


A valid Swiss work permit or Swiss or EU-25/EFTA citizenship is required for this position.
This role requires up to 10% of business travel time.

Title: Data Operations Analyst
Description:
Our client is seeking a Data Operations Analyst with a strong supply chain mindset to support regulated, data-driven operational processes. This role sits at the intersection of data operations, regulatory compliance, and supply chain execution, contributing to the reliable launch and supply of products to customers. The position focuses on day-to-day operational excellence, issue resolution, and continuous improvement in a regulated environment, while collaborating closely with cross‑functional and senior stakeholders.




Responsibilities

Govern and maintain the client’s internal regulatory applications and approval databases
Execute data operations and data quality processes, including validation, error handling, audit support, and compliance-related activities (e.g., NCs and CAPAs)
Support supply chain teams by investigating issues, resolving data or regulatory blocks, and managing order holds when required
Act as a key point of contact between different internal and external counterparts, including third‑party suppliers
Provide project-related support, contributing to operational improvements, system enhancements, and framework deployments
Prepare and maintain documentation such as SOPs, work instructions, and data quality standards, and support training activities
Participate in and, at times, lead operational, governance, and review meetings
Collaborate on metrics, dashboards, and reporting to support data transparency and operational performance
Manage senior stakeholders effectively, demonstrating the ability to challenge, influence, and push back where necessary
Continuously learn and adapt to new tools, technologies, and processes to improve data operations outcomes




Must-Haves

Experience in supply chain, data operations, master data, or a similar operational environment
Strong understanding of regulated data environments and approval or compliance-driven processes
Hands-on experience working with data systems, databases, or ERP platforms (e.g., SAP, JDE, or similar)
Strong knowledge of Excel (VLOOKUP, Pivot Table,...)
Ability to analyze issues, identify root causes, and drive resolution in a structured manner
Advanced organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast‑changing, matrix environment
Experience with S4/HANA is a plus
Experience in MedTech industry is a plus




Soft Skills

Strong communication skills and confidence working with senior stakeholders
Proactive mindset with a high level of ownership and accountability
Eagerness to learn, openness to new technologies, and comfort asking questions
Resilience and confidence to push back constructively when needed
Strong time management skills and the ability to work effectively in diverse, multicultural teams




Please note:

This role is remote but you have to be located in Europe
You need to be/willing to set-up as a freelancer
Available immediately or 1 month notice period maximum

Title: Reference Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description

Reference Data Analyst

______________________________________________________________________

Location: Zurich, Switzerland

Type: Full-time

Department: Financial Markets

_______________________________________________________________________

Summary

As a Reference Data Analyst you will be responsible for the accurate creation, maintenance, and governance of static and dynamic reference data across multiple financial products and systems.

This role plays a critical part in supporting collateral management operations by ensuring data integrity, timely updates, and alignment with regulatory and operational standards.

Responsibilities


Maintain and validate reference data for counterparties, securities, and agreements used in collateral management systems and workflows.
Set up accounts in Collateral Management Systems, ensuring alignment with CSAs, ISDA, and SMA agreements.
Collaborate with middle office, risk, and legal teams to onboard new counterparties, secure approvals and update records.
Liaise with external vendors (e.g., Bloomberg, Markit, DTCC) and internal stakeholders to source and verify data.
Initiate and coordinate SSIs setup requests and coordinate with Ops teams and stakeholders.
Address queries related to collateral accounts, SSIs setup.
Plan and track client onboarding schedules, highlighting dependencies and risks.
Maintain trackers and provide regular status updates in meetings and calls.
Ensure timely coordination and escalation of issues/risks.
Support additional or ad-hoc requests and projects.
Adhere to operational controls (e.g. SOPs, SLAs, and KPIs).
Participate in data quality initiatives, including exception reporting, root cause analysis, and remediation.
Contribute to automation and process improvement efforts using tools such as Alteryx, Xceptor, or internal workflow engines.
Ensure compliance with internal controls, audit requirements, and regulatory standards.


Eligibility Requirements


Ideally, 1–5 years of experience in reference data management, preferably within collateral, middle office, or securities operations.
Good understanding of financial instruments (e.g., OTC derivatives, equities, fixed income, FX) and collateral lifecycle.
Ideally, familiarity with collateral management platforms (e.g., AcadiaSoft, TriResolve, Murex) and data governance tools.
Proficiency in Excel (pivot tables, lookups), with exposure to SQL or data visualisation tools being a plus.
Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.
Ability to work under pressure and manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Preferred Qualifications
Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Economics, or a related field.
Knowledge of regulatory frameworks impacting collateral and data.
Experience with reconciliation tools and exception management workflows.
Languages
German and/or English
Work Permit
Must possess a Swiss Work Permit or a EU Passport

About EClerx

eClerx is a leading provider of productized services, bringing together people, technology and domain expertise to amplify business results.

The firm provides business process management, automation, and analytics services to a number of Fortune 2000 enterprises, including some of the world’s leading financial services, communications, retail, fashion, media & entertainment, manufacturing, travel & leisure, and technology companies. Incorporated in 2000, eClerx is traded on both the Bombay and National Stock Exchanges of India. The firm employs more than 19,000 people across Australia, Canada, France, Germany, Switzerland, Eqypt. India, Italy, Netherlands, Peru, Philippines, Singapore, Thailand, the UK, and the USA.

Title: Data Collector
Description:
Get paid to submit photos and screenshots of expired loyalty cards, event tickets, gift cards, and coupons with barcodes. Simple task, remote, global participation.




We are hiring freelance contributors worldwide to help us collect real photos and screenshots of expired cards, passes, and tickets with barcodes for a global research project.

This is a remote, flexible, task-based project where you submit images you already own or can easily capture.




📌 What you will submit

You will upload photos or screenshots of:

Membership / Loyalty cards (gym, library, store rewards, etc.)
Event tickets / passes (concerts, sports, exhibitions, conferences, etc.)
Gift cards (expired or used only)
Coupons (expired or already used)

**Every item must contain a visible barcode.

***Only real data — no AI-generated or internet images allowed.




📌 Basic Requirements

Must be 18+ years old
Must own the cards/tickets you submit
Cards/passes must be expired, used, or zero balance
Barcode must be clearly visible and readable
Images must be original and not downloaded from the internet




📌 Image Guidelines (Simple)

Take clear photos in good lighting
Show the full card/pass — no cropping
Different backgrounds encouraged (table, hand, wall, indoor/outdoor, etc.)
Screenshots must show merchant name + barcode
Do NOT use Apple Wallet screenshots
Accepted formats: JPG, JPEG, PNG, HEIC

📌 Do's:

Barcode should be visible and not obstructed by any element
Merchant name should be visible along with barcode, else capture other side where Merchant name is given
All Physical assets needs to be in jpg, jpeg, png and heic extensions only
Location on Phone should be switched on to capture EXIF Data (Latitude and Longitude)
Do not edit Photos post capturing which will remove details of exif
Physical assets are only Photo Images and not Screenshots or picture of picture
The orientation of the physical image should be straight and not curved or bended

📌 Dont's:

Photos captured should be strictly from Physical cards
No paper cards or printed cards are allowed
Strictly avoid Web scraping or fabrication of assets
Assets created using generated AI or derived from synthetic data will be rejected
Exif data is mandatory and any Photo with no exif data will be rejected




🌍 Target Countries

We are especially looking for contributors from:

China, Taiwan, Hong Kong, Japan, South Korea, Vietnam, Turkey, Israel, Thailand, Malaysia, India, Brazil, Portugal, France, Germany, Spain, Italy, Sweden, Norway, Denmark, Finland, Netherlands, Egypt, Saudi Arabia, Jordan, and more.




💰 Payment

Paid per accepted asset
More valid submissions = higher total payout




📩 How To Apply

Step 1 – Join Communication & Support Channel - https://discord.gg/gtRKfkFPPU

Step 2 – Connect with Admin Namrita on the Communication channel to get project execution details and Data Submission app link




**Spots are limited per country, so early registration is recommended.

Title: Data Analyst / Cloud Migration
Description:
Descrizione Del Lavoro

Our organization is launching a strategic initiative to migrate its data infrastructure from an on-premise Oracle Database environment to Google Cloud Platform (GCP). The project involves consolidating legacy data warehouses, re-architecting ETL/ELT pipelines, and adopting cloud-native services such as BigQuery, Cloud Storage, and Dataflow.

The selected Data Analyst will be a key contributor across every phase of the migration lifecycle: from as-is data analysis of source systems, through post-migration validation, to supporting business teams in their transition to new cloud-based analytics tools.

Key Responsibilities

Data Analysis & Mapping


Inventory and document Oracle source data (schemas, tables, stored procedures, views)
Perform data profiling to identify anomalies, duplicates, and inconsistencies
Define logical data mappings from Oracle to BigQuery for each data domain
Collaborate with data engineers on the definition of transformation rules


Validation & Quality Assurance


Design and implement data reconciliation tests for pre- and post-migration phases
Develop SQL / BigQuery SQL queries to compare source and target datasets
Produce data quality reports covering completeness, accuracy, and consistency metrics
Log and track data anomalies using issue-tracking tools


Migration Support


Actively participate in sprint planning and stand-ups with technical and business teams
Support the migration of legacy reports into GCP-native tools (Looker, Looker Studio)
Contribute to the definition of data governance standards and cloud best practices
Document data transformation processes for knowledge transfer purposes


Personal Profile & Soft Skills


Strong analytical mindset and attention to detail
Ability to communicate complex data findings to non-technical stakeholders
Operational autonomy and a proactive approach to problem-solving
Good time management in multi-task and project-driven environments
Team player: comfortable working in cross-functional IT/Business teams
Flexibility and adaptability in contexts of technological change
Proficient English (written and spoken, minimum B2 level)


Education


Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, or a related field
Specialization courses in data engineering, cloud computing, or analytics are a plus

Title: Data Analyste
Description:
Le·la Data Analyst a pour mission principale d’assurer la production de flux de données et l’évolution des analyses métiers.
Il·elle exploite les données issues des entrepôts existants afin de soutenir la prise de décision, optimiser les processus et produire des tableaux de bord pertinents et cohérents.






Formation et expérience




Diplôme universitaire (Bachelor ou équivalent) en statistiques, mathématiques, informatique ou domaine connexe.


Une expérience avec les modèles sémantiques et de présentation constitue un atout.




Qualités personnelles




Esprit analytique et rigoureux, orienté solutions.


Capacité à comprendre les besoins métiers et à les challenger.


Autonomie, dynamisme, sens des priorités.


Capacité à travailler sous pression et en équipe.


Aptitude à communiquer clairement les résultats d’analyse à différents interlocuteurs.


Ouverture au changement et adaptabilité.






Requirements



Maîtrise de Microsoft Power BI Desktop et Power BI Report Server.


Excellente connaissance des bases de données et des plateformes de virtualisation de données.


Maîtrise du langage SQL pour la manipulation et l’analyse des données.


Solides compétences en visualisation de données et présentation de résultats.


Bonne compréhension des concepts statistiques de base (distributions, régressions, tests d’hypothèses…).


Capacité à formaliser des exigences techniques et des concepts de traitement de données.


Aptitude à résoudre des problèmes complexes liés à l’analyse et à la cohérence des indicateurs.




Compétences importantes




Bonne maîtrise de l’outil DataChain pour la virtualisation de données.


Capacité à concevoir et présenter des tableaux de bord clairs et exploitables.


Capacité à identifier des tendances et formuler des recommandations concrètes.


Rigueur dans la documentation et la traçabilité des processus analytiques.




Compétences optionnelles




Expérience dans la mise en œuvre de services numériques intégrant des processus de bout en bout.

Title: Career Starter Data Science (m/w/d)
Description:
Beweg die Schweiz mit uns.

Career Starter Data Science (m/w/d)

Bern

01.09.2026

60-100%

101958

Darauf kannst du dich freuen.

Daten sind voll dein Ding? Dann bist du bei der SBB genau richtig: Denn hier entstehen sie überall – im Energienetz, aus Messsensoren zum Zustand unserer Fahrzeugflotte oder mit Kameras zur Schadensdetektion an unseren Gleisen. Als Career Starter Data Science bei der SBB vertiefst du während 18 Monaten dein Wissen in Data Engineering, Data Science, MLOps und AI-Engineering. Dank des Rahmenprogramms und der Zeit für Weiterbildungen baust du dir Bahnwissen auf, damit du mit deinen technischen Skills wirkungsvolle Use Cases umsetzen kannst. Mehr zu unseren Career Starter Programmen findest du hier.

Das kannst du bewegen.


In drei sechsmonatigen Förderstellen arbeitest du als integriertes Teammitglied von IT- und Datenteams an unterschiedlichen Herausforderungen der SBB. Dabei lernst du, wie man Daten gewinnbringend aufbereiten und einsetzen kann - sei es als Datenprodukt, gezielte Auswertung oder Machine-Learning-Modell.
Mit einem modernen Cloud Data Warehouse Stack bereitest du Daten aus verschiedenen Datenquellen zu nutzbaren Datenprodukten auf.
Du entwickelst Machine-Learning-Modelle und begleitest diese von der Prototyp-Phase bis hin zu möglichen produktiven Einsätzen (MLOps).
Als technische Ansprechperson arbeitest du eng mit Fachbereichen zusammen, analysierst AI-Anwendungsfälle und unterstützt beim Erwartungsmanagement in Bezug auf Machbarkeit, Nutzen und Reifegrad von AI-Lösungen.
Du treibst das Verständnis für das Potenzial von Daten im Unternehmen aktiv voran, indem du dich beispielsweise in der Data Science Community engagierst oder Schulungen zu Daten und AI gestaltest und durchführst.
Dank verschiedener Weiterbildungen und Besichtigungen (zum Beispiel von Kraftwerken oder Rangierbahnhöfen) entwickelst du ein Verständnis für das komplexe System Bahn. Dies erlaubt dir, die verschiedenen Zusammenhänge zu verstehen und mit deinem technischen Wissen die richtigen fachlichen Herausforderungen zu lösen.




Das bringst du mit.


Durch hohe Eigeninitiative und Leistungsbereitschaft zeichnest du dich aus.
Du verfügst über ein starkes analytisches Denkvermögen und kannst aus Daten fundierte Erkenntnisse und Handlungsempfehlungen ableiten.
Als Teamplayer:in mit ausgeprägten Kommunikationsfähigkeiten und einer Faszination für die Mobilität freust du dich, immer wieder neue Herausforderungen anzunehmen und dich in verschiedene Teams einzuarbeiten.
Einen Hochschulabschluss (Uni, FH, Bachelor/Master) in einem MINT-Bereich (Mathematik, Informatik, Naturwissenschaften oder Technik), Volkswirtschaft oder Wirtschaftsinformatik bringst du mit.
Du hast gute Programmierkenntnisse und bringst Erfahrung in Statistik, Datenanalyse und Machine Learning mit. Idealerweise bist du mit dem Data-Science-Stack von Python vertraut (pandas, numpy, PyTorch / TensorFlow / JAX, scikit-learn etc.). Erfahrungen mit Git, Docker und CI/CD sind sehr gewünscht.
Sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse (mindestens B2) und gute Englischkenntnisse (B1) bringst du für diese Funktion mit.




Dein Lohn Und Deine Benefits.

Wir haben ein faires und fortschrittliches Lohnsystem und setzen uns aktiv für Lohngleichheit ein. Deshalb sind weiterführende und detaillierte Informationen zum Lohnsystem bei der SBB transparent einsehbar. Diese Stelle befindet sich im Anforderungsniveau I, Regionalzulage Stufe 1. Mehr zu Lohn und Benefits.

Damit gelingt der Einstieg.

Dass du mit uns die Schweiz bewegen willst, ist für uns die grösste Motivation. Darum verzichten wir bei dieser Stelle auf ein Motivationsschreiben. Falls du uns deine Motivation dennoch mitteilen möchtest, hast du in unserem Bewerbungstool nach wie vor die Möglichkeit dazu. Grosses machen ist einfacher per du. Das beginnt bereits bei deiner Bewerbung.

Für diese Stelle nehmen wir keine Dossiers von Personalvermittlern entgegen.

Zur Stelle

Title: Video Data Reviewer - Remote
Description:
Job Title: Video Data Reviewer

Job Type: Contractor

Location: Remote

Job Summary

Join our customers team as a Video Data Reviewer, youll apply your expertise to help train next-generation AI systems. Your work will shape how models learn, reason, and perform through high-quality, real-world input. No prior experience in AI is required; your domain knowledge is what matters.

Key Responsibilities


Review and refine model-generated video annotations to ensure accuracy and precision in describing hand movements and object interactions.
Validate and correct action timing, ensuring boundaries align with the start and end of each labeled activity.
Assess and edit captions for atomicity, confirming each label describes a single, independent action.
Verify correct identification of left versus right hand usage in all annotated segments.
Flag low-quality, ambiguous, or incomplete labels for rework based on project standards.
Maintain consistent labeling quality by adhering to established annotation rubrics and structured review pipelines.
Provide actionable feedback to help enhance annotation models, and collaborate with team leads to resolve edge cases.


Required Skills And Qualifications


Keen visual discrimination skills and high attention to detail, especially when analyzing repetitive video content.
Ability to maintain focus and deliver reliable quality throughout long video review sessions.
Comfort with web-based annotation tools and time-coded video platforms.
Strong written and verbal communication skills, ensuring clear and concise captioning and feedback.
Demonstrated process adherence and willingness to follow detailed guidelines and incorporate prompt feedback.
Reliable high-speed internet connection and a computer capable of smooth video playback.
Ability to work productively and autonomously while meeting daily throughput and quality targets.


Preferred Qualifications


Experience in video annotation, labeling, transcription, or content moderation roles.
Familiarity with annotation software such as CVAT, Labelbox, Scale, V7, or Encord.
Background in fields requiring precise observation of human movement (e.g., occupational therapy, kinesiology, sign language interpretation, sports analysis).

Analytics Engineer / Data Analyst – Smart Metering (H/F)

Dans un contexte de croissance de l’activité Data, l’équipe Data métier Smart Metering souhaite renforcer ses capacités d’analyse et de modélisation.

Nous recherchons un profil hybride Analytics Engineer / Data Analyst, capable de combiner une forte expertise technique en modélisation de données avec une réelle sensibilité métier.

Ce poste s’adresse à un profil orienté analytics et business, et non à un Data Engineer ou Data Scientist purement technique.

Mission

Vous intervenez au cœur des problématiques data liées au Smart Metering, avec pour objectif de transformer les besoins métiers en données exploitables, fiables et compréhensibles.

Vos Principales Missions Sont Les Suivantes


Concevoir et maintenir des modèles de données analytiques à l’aide de dbt (Core)
Réaliser des analyses avancées, incluant l’analyse de séries temporelles et l’exploration de données
Construire des indicateurs métiers pertinents et assurer leur fiabilité
Traduire les besoins business en solutions data concrètes et opérationnelles
Travailler dans un environnement Microsoft Fabric, avec une première expérience appréciée
Collaborer étroitement avec les équipes métiers afin de comprendre les usages, challenger les besoins et proposer des analyses à valeur ajoutée
Garantir la qualité, la cohérence et la documentation des modèles et des indicateurs développés


Profil


Expérience confirmée d’au moins 3 ans sur des missions similaires (modélisation de données, analytics, construction d’indicateurs)
Excellente maîtrise de SQL avancé
Très bonne pratique de dbt (Core) pour la modélisation analytique
Solides compétences en analyse de séries temporelles et en analyse exploratoire
Une première expérience sur Microsoft Fabric est fortement appréciée
Une expérience avérée dans le domaine des compteurs intelligents, de préférence au sein de services publics ou du secteur de l'énergie.
Toute expérience préalable dans le domaine du DataOps serait un atout, notamment une connaissance des infrastructures de données, de l'architecture informatique et des environnements de données opérationnels.
Capacité à interagir avec les métiers et à traduire des enjeux business en solutions data
Esprit analytique, rigueur et autonomie
Bon sens de la communication et goût pour le travail collaboratif


Seules les candidatures des citoyens suisses et de l'UE seront prises en compte / Nos postes sont accessibles aux personnes en situation de handicap / Télétravail possible

Title: Senior Product analyst
Description:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Senior Product Analyst in Switzerland.

This role offers an exciting opportunity to shape product decisions for large-scale, global social discovery platforms that connect millions of users worldwide. You will play a key role in transforming raw data into actionable insights that drive product growth, user engagement, and business strategy. Working in a fully remote, international environment, you will collaborate closely with product managers, engineers, and marketing teams to define, test, and optimize product features. The role combines deep analytical work with strategic influence, allowing you to directly impact product direction through data-driven decision-making. You will be responsible for identifying trends, uncovering opportunities, and validating hypotheses through experimentation and advanced analytics. This is an ideal position for a data-driven professional who thrives in fast-paced, consumer-focused digital environments.

Accountabilities


Build and maintain automated data collection pipelines from multiple heterogeneous sources and develop centralized BI dashboards for product monitoring.
Analyze core product and business metrics to identify anomalies, trends, and opportunities for growth or optimization.
Design, execute, and evaluate A/B tests, ensuring methodological accuracy and reliable interpretation of results.
Provide analytical support for product hypothesis generation and feature development initiatives.
Conduct deep-dive analysis of large datasets to uncover hidden patterns, dependencies, and actionable growth insights.
Collaborate with product, engineering, and marketing teams to translate data insights into strategic product decisions.
Monitor marketing and product performance metrics to support user acquisition, engagement, and retention strategies.
Ensure data integrity, consistency, and accuracy across reporting systems and analytical tools.


Requirements


Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in a quantitative or technical field such as Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, or Engineering.
Strong knowledge of statistical analysis, probability theory, and experimental design.
Proficiency in data analysis tools such as SQL, Python, and Excel.
Extensive experience in designing, executing, and analyzing A/B tests in product environments.
Strong understanding of key product and marketing metrics, including user engagement, retention, conversion, and LTV.
Experience working with large datasets and building meaningful dashboards or visualizations.
Strong analytical thinking with the ability to translate complex data into clear business insights.
Excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
Ability to work independently in a remote, global, and cross-functional environment.
Strong communication skills to present findings to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.


Benefits


Fully remote work opportunity with global flexibility.
28 calendar days of paid annual vacation.
7 additional wellness days per year for rest and personal needs.
Annual bonuses for successful employee referrals (up to $5,000).
50% reimbursement for professional training, courses, and international conferences.
Corporate discounts for English language learning programs.
Health-related financial support up to $1,000 per year for insurance or medical expenses.
Home office or coworking space support with equipment reimbursement up to $1,000 every 3 years.
Access to internal gamified reward system with redeemable bonuses for perks and team activities.
Fully remote, international, and highly collaborative work environment.


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Video Data Annotator - Remote
Description:
Job Title: Video Data Reviewer

Job Type: Contractor

Location: Remote

Job Summary

Join our customers team as a Video Data Reviewer, youll apply your expertise to help train next-generation AI systems. Your work will shape how models learn, reason, and perform through high-quality, real-world input. No prior experience in AI is required; your domain knowledge is what matters.

Key Responsibilities


Review and refine model-generated video annotations to ensure accuracy and precision in describing hand movements and object interactions.
Validate and correct action timing, ensuring boundaries align with the start and end of each labeled activity.
Assess and edit captions for atomicity, confirming each label describes a single, independent action.
Verify correct identification of left versus right hand usage in all annotated segments.
Flag low-quality, ambiguous, or incomplete labels for rework based on project standards.
Maintain consistent labeling quality by adhering to established annotation rubrics and structured review pipelines.
Provide actionable feedback to help enhance annotation models, and collaborate with team leads to resolve edge cases.


Required Skills And Qualifications


Keen visual discrimination skills and high attention to detail, especially when analyzing repetitive video content.
Ability to maintain focus and deliver reliable quality throughout long video review sessions.
Comfort with web-based annotation tools and time-coded video platforms.
Strong written and verbal communication skills, ensuring clear and concise captioning and feedback.
Demonstrated process adherence and willingness to follow detailed guidelines and incorporate prompt feedback.
Reliable high-speed internet connection and a computer capable of smooth video playback.
Ability to work productively and autonomously while meeting daily throughput and quality targets.


Preferred Qualifications


Experience in video annotation, labeling, transcription, or content moderation roles.
Familiarity with annotation software such as CVAT, Labelbox, Scale, V7, or Encord.
Background in fields requiring precise observation of human movement (e.g., occupational therapy, kinesiology, sign language interpretation, sports analysis).

Title: Data Engineer (H/F)
Company: CBTW IT & Technology / Positive Thinking Company
Description:
Chez 𝗖𝗕𝗧𝗪 (Collaboration Betters The World), entreprise mondiale de conseil en technologie, la collaboration est la pierre angulaire de l’innovation et du succès, et le fondement de tout ce que nous mettons en œuvre.

Notre mission : transformer les entreprises et les communautés pour un monde meilleur.

Présents dans plus de 20 pays et 35 villes, nous allions une expertise mondiale avec une compréhension locale des enjeux de nos clients.




Au sein de la Service Line "Data Analytics & IA", nous développons une approche qui vise à accompagner nos clients dans la définition de leurs stratégies en matière de Data Science et d’IA, d’Analytics et Business Intelligence, de Data et plateformes. L’objectif est de les accompagner à chaque étape de leurs parcours afin de les aider à libérer le potentiel de leurs données.




Que vas-tu faire ?




Concrètement au sein de l'expertise Data Engineering, tu seras en charge de :




1. Conception & Architecture

Concevoir des architectures Data scalables et sécurisées : Data Lake, Data Warehouse.
Réaliser des estimations de coûts et comparer objectivement différentes technologies et architectures.
Participer aux choix technologiques en fonction des enjeux métiers et contraintes techniques.
Définir et promouvoir les bonnes pratiques d’ingénierie et d’architecture Data.




2. Développement & Industrialisation

Construire et industrialiser des pipelines batch et temps réel.
Implémenter des pratiques CI/CD, observabilité, gouvernance et data quality.







Quel est ton profil ?




Tu as une expérience significative de plus de 7 ans en data engineering.
Tu es issu d’une formation dans le domaine de la Data niveau Bac +5/Ecole d'ingénieur
Tu détiens une expertise sur une Data Platform moderne idéalement Data Bricks ou Microsoft Fabric
Tu maîtrises un cloud public (Azure, AWS ou GCP).
Tu as de très bonnes compétences en SQL et Python.
Tu disposes de solides connaissances en architecture Data moderne.
Tu as une forte aisance relationnelle notamment pour l'animation d'atelier variés (conception, ateliers agiles, retros et cadrages).




Notre processus de recrutement :

1ère étape : Rencontre avec un membre de l’équipe RH pour introduire le poste, l’environnement de travail et comprendre tes motivations ;

2ème étape : Rencontre avec les équipes de la Service Line pour faire connaissance mais aussi te challenger techniquement ;

3ème étape : Rencontre avec un membre de la direction et l’un de nos sales pour te challenger sur ta compréhension de CBTW, le partage de valeurs communes et ta motivation pour nous rejoindre.




Ce que nous te proposons :

Collaborer au sein d'une entreprise internationale dynamique et ouverte d'esprit où tes idées sont entendues et valorisées ;
Accéder à des perspectives d’évolution professionnelle tout au long de ta carrière via des formations et des certifications ;
Bénéficier d'un suivi de carrière sur mesure avec ton référent RH et des points réguliers avec ton Business Manager lors de tes missions ;
Intégrer des communautés te permettant de partager des centres d'intérêts communs ou expertises techniques ;
Créer du lien avec des collaborateurs passionnés à travers différents évènements internes (afterworks, soirées annuelles, séminaires, …).




Nous avons hâte d'échanger avec toi !

Title: Internship Global Master Data Analyst 80-100% (m/f/d)
Description:
Switzerland, Visp

Today, Lonza is a global leader in life sciences operating across five continents. While we work in science, there’s no magic formula to how we do it. Our greatest scientific solution is talented people working together, devising ideas that help businesses to help people. In exchange, we let our people own their careers. Their ideas, big and small, genuinely improve the world. And that’s the kind of work we want to be part of.

What You Will Do

The Master Data department in Visp is a global team responsible for the development of master data processes and standards that are to be followed by the whole organization. As an intern, you will play an active role in:


Perform various data analyses and reports to support Master data or Business projects
Assisting with SAP Master Data mass maintenance and migration efforts
Gathering feedback from global and local Subject Matter Experts (SMEs) across the organization
Defining functional and technical requirements for the development or enhancement of Master Data processes
Updating and improving training materials, and delivering hands-on training sessions to teams across Lonza


As a part of the Global Master Data team, you will be able to learn SAP Materials Management (MM), Production & Planning (PP) and Sales & Distribution (SD) modules, key business processes as well as SAP data structures. Overall, you will interact with many different departments and experts across the company worldwide.

What We Are Looking For


A Bachelor’s or Master’s degree (or equivalent experience) in data science, computer science, business information technology, or mathematics
Solid understanding of data structures or hands-on experience with databases
Proficiency in English; German and other languages are a valuable plus
A passion for optimizing processes, improving IT tools, and exploring innovative solutions
You are interested in being a reliable team member in a multi-cultural environment.


About Lonza

At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.

Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.

Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now.

Title: Tracking Specialist
Description:
fifty-five is a new kind of data company that helps brands transform with data and AI to improve their marketing operations through a combination of specialized consultancy and technology services.

As the strategic data pillar of The Brandtech Group, we offer services that combine strategy consulting, cloud services, media consulting and customer experience.

We are made up of over +320 digital experts. Digital consultants, tracking and media specialists, engineers and data scientists all work in close collaboration to provide senior-level marketing consultancy and technical support to our clients, in every type of industry, all around the world.

As a partner to advertisers from data collection to activation and exploitation, we help organizations become truly omnichannel entities that control the efficiency of their digital ecosystem and its synergies with the physical world.

Headquartered in Paris, we operate across 3 time zones from our 10 offices, located in Paris, London, Geneva, Milan, Shanghai, Hong Kong, Shenzhen, Taipei, Singapore and New York. fifty-five attaches particular importance to the well-being of its employees, which enabled it to be included in the Best Workplaces France ranking in 2018.

After an intensive training period, you will join a team of experts whose mission is to ensure the quality and reliability of data collection for fifty-five's clients.


Responsibilities :



You will rely on your technical expertise in web analytics solutions and tag management systems (TMS) to carry out a variety of missions for our clients.


Your role will be to:

Implement the technical phase of web/mobile or app tracking deployment for the client, from requirements gathering to the effective collection of data, for which you guarantee quality and reliability
Manage projects directly with the client (ensuring progress, scheduling, testing strategy, and deliverables) related to Analytics and Media data collection
Share your knowledge and expertise with clients and internally
Maintain constant communication with key stakeholders on the client and integrator sides
Develop strong operational expertise (TMS & Analytics)
Identify and develop new business opportunities in collaboration with the consultants in charge of the account
Contribute to the definition of the company's internal products and tools
Participate in shaping and positioning fifty-five's technological offering




Requirements

Education and Experience :



With a Master's degree (Bac+5) from an engineering or business school, you have 1 to 2 years of experience in Tracking / Measurement / Web Analytics project management.


Skills and Interests :


Fluent in French & English and excellent communication, oral and written
Project management experience working with both digital business teams and technical teams (knowledge of major tools is a plus)
Main web analytics tools (Adobe Analytics, Google Analytics, AT Internet, etc.)
Tag Management Systems (GTM, Launch, Tealium, Commanders Act, etc.)
Knowledge of JavaScript (understanding and ability to develop simple logic) and web technologies in general
Strong analytical mindset
Strong interest in technology and the internet sector
Experience in mobile environments is a plus
Knowledge of CMP (Consent Management Platforms) is a plus
Strong ability to propose solutions perfectly aligned with business needs

This policy applies to all terms and conditions.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
K&K Talents is an international recruiting agency that has been providing technical resources in the European region since 1993. This position is with one of our clients in Switzerland who is actively hiring candidates to expand their teams.

Position : Data Engineer

Location : Zurich, Switzerland(Hybrid)

Type : Permanent

Key responsibilities

• Design, build, and maintain ETL processes and data delivery pipelines for downstream applications.

• Create final data deliveries, primarily files, to be consumed by internal application teams.

• Work with databases, source data, transformation logic, and target delivery formats.

• Query databases, analyze source data, and optimize SQL or ETL logic where needed.

• Support data migration and integration activities within the ROC Core program.

• Work with internal application clients to understand data requirements, issues, and delivery expectations.

• Translate business and technical requirements into practical ETL and data delivery solutions.

• Investigate data quality issues, mapping questions, transformation logic, and delivery problems.

• Collaborate with software developers, business analysts, project managers, and upstream data providers.

• Contribute to documentation, data mappings, delivery specifications, and operational handover materials.




Required experience

• Strong experience as a Data Engineer, ETL Engineer, Integration Engineer, Migration Engineer, or similar role.

Title: Applied ML/Computer Vision Intern at a YC Backed Startup
Description:
About Cerrion

At Cerrion, we’re building the future of AI-enabled manufacturing. Headquartered in Zurich and backed by Y Combinator and top Silicon Valley investors, we’re building a platform to deploy video AI Agents that can see what’s happening on the production floor, automatically spot problems, and instantly intervene to keep lines running smoothly, at scale. Our technology is live in factories across 15 countries, helping leading manufacturers in glass, beverage, food, CPG, building materials and wood cut downtime by up to 50%. We’ve reached an exciting inflection point — and we’re accelerating fast.


The Role

We’re hiring an Applied ML / Computer Vision Intern to help improve real-world vision systems deployed across more than 50 factories. You’ll dig into model and data failures end-to-end — from datasets, labels, and augmentations to training dynamics and evaluation — identify the root causes of recurring errors, and implement focused fixes in Python. Working alongside our research and operations teams, you’ll follow an established process, propose practical improvements, and validate them with targeted evaluations. This role is a great fit for someone with a computer vision and Python background who enjoys tracing problems through the full ML stack and turning findings into measurable system improvements.


Your responsibilities
Model and Data Failure Analysis: Investigate failure cases across datasets, labels, augmentations, sampling strategy, and training dynamics. Identify recurring error patterns, trace likely root causes, and propose practical fixes.
Computer Vision System Improvement: Use computer vision knowledge to suggest changes to the data pipeline, training setup, evaluation process, or model behavior based on findings from experiments and failure analysis.
Python Implementation: Implement proposed changes in Python, including data processing scripts, evaluation tools, dataset filters, training configuration changes, visualization utilities, and experiment tracking improvements.
Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work with operations and annotation teams to understand domain-specific edge cases, refine labeling guidelines, adjust processes, and document findings clearly.
Documentation & Process: Document experiments, failure modes, decisions, and process updates so the team can reuse the workflow and build on what you learned.

Desired Skills and Experience
Computer Vision Experience: Hands-on experience with computer vision tasks such as classification and detection, including training and evaluating models on real-world image data.
Debugging Mindset: Ability to debug model performance using targeted evaluations rather than only looking at aggregate metrics, and to trace issues through data, labels, augmentations, and training behavior.
Proficient Programming Skills: Good Python skills and familiarity with common ML/CV libraries such as PyTorch, comfortable reading existing code and improving it incrementally.
Communication & Collaboration: Clear communication skills and willingness to interact with operations, annotation, and QA teams to align on domain-specific edge cases and labeling guidelines.
Documentation Discipline: Ability to document experiments, failure modes, decisions, and process updates so that findings are reusable across the team.

Preferred Qualifications
Experiment Tracking: Familiarity with experiment tracking tools such as ClearML, Weights & Biases, or equivalents.
Dashboards & Visualisation: Experience building dashboards, visualizations, or evaluation reports to surface failure patterns and track model behavior.
Real-World CV Systems: Previous work on industrial, robotics, manufacturing, safety, medical, or other real-world computer vision systems.

Why Cerrion
Ownership & impact: Work on hard, real-world vision problems that directly improve manufacturing for some of the world’s largest producers. Your contributions will run in factories across 15 countries.
Growth path: Learn end-to-end applied computer vision — from data and labels through training and evaluation — by working alongside an experienced R&D team on production systems.
Compensation: Competitive intern compensation and comprehensive support.
Location: Modern office in the heart of Zurich; enjoy the quality of life of Switzerland and the dynamism of a high-growth startup.

If you’re excited about tracing computer vision problems end-to-end, sharpening real-world models with focused experiments, and seeing your work deployed in factories across the globe, we’d love to meet you.

Title: Business Analyst CFAO (H/F)
Description:
Pour notre département Systèmes d'Information, nous recherchons divers Business Analysts "CFAO" dont la mission principale sera d'avoir la responsabilité fonctionnelle d'une partie du périmètre applicatif répondant aux besoins métier sur les domaines des outils soit de la Programmation Contrôle (CAI) soit de la Modélisation de maquette numérique (CAD).

Responsabilités


Identifier et analyser les besoins du métier soit pour la partie CAI soit pour la partie CAD
Proposer des solutions logicielles pour répondre aux besoins en accord avec la stratégie SI à déployer (rationalisation et intégration)
Assurer la chaine numérique du cycle de développement produit et les évolutions logiciels en partenariat avec nos fournisseurs
Assurer le maintient opérationnel des applications dont vous êtes responsable en partenariat avec les autres acteurs interne (Infrastructure / Sécurité / Réseau / Poste Client)


Profil


Ecole d'ingénieur avec une spécialisation sur le périmètre Industriel
5 ans d'ancienneté dans le domaine de l'administration logiciel
Maitriser soit l'application METROLOG (Metrologic Group) pour la partie CAI soit l'application CREO (PTC) pour la partie CAD
Compétences sur l'intégration logiciel dans un environnement IT Industriel
Bonnes connaissances de l'anglais (B2)
Bonne capacité de synthèse et de gestion du stress
Communiquant et conciliant


Nous vous offrons un environnement stimulant, d’excellentes conditions de travail, des solutions de flexibilisation du temps de travail et des prestations sociales de premier ordre.

Nous vous remercions d'adresser un dossier de candidature complet (CV, lettre de motivation, certificats et diplômes).

Title: Document Controller
Description:
Domaines d’activité : Énergie, industrie de machines spéciales.

Taux d'activité : 80 à 100%

Votre Mission

Nous recherchons un/une Document Controller afin d’assister les chefs de projets ainsi que l’équipe Assurance Produit.

Dans cette fonction, vous jouez un rôle clé dans la gestion et le suivi des documents liés aux projets. Vous assurez la qualité, la conformité et la bonne circulation des documents entre les équipes internes et les clients.

Ce Que Vous Réalisez


Assurer la gestion et le suivi d’un volume important de documents techniques et administratifs
Vérifier la conformité documentaire : pages de signature, formatage, versioning, marquage confidentiel, etc.
Préparer et transmettre les documents aux clients via les plateformes dédiées
Garantir le respect des délais d’envoi et des échéances documentaires
Assurer le contrôle qualité des documents avant diffusion
Réceptionner et suivre les commentaires clients
Coordonner les mises à jour documentaires avec les équipes internes en tenant compte des retours et validations
Maintenir une traçabilité claire des versions et des échanges documentaires
Participer à la communication avec les clients et les équipes projets (emails et appels pour suivi administratif)
Collaborer avec l’ensemble des chefs de projets et des équipes internes


Ce que vous apportez :


Vous êtes titulaire d’un CFC ou titre jugé équivalent
Première expérience en administration, coordination ou gestion documentaire appréciée
Excellente rigueur et grand sens du détail
Aisance relationnelle et capacité à communiquer efficacement avec des interlocuteurs variés
Maitrise du français et de l’anglais (minimum B2), à l’oral comme à l’écrit
Capacité à gérer des volumes importants de documents dans un environnement exigeant
À l’aise avec les outils informatiques, les plateformes documentaires et la suite Office
Bon sens de l’organisation et des priorités
Intérêt et curiosité pour les disciplines techniques
La maîtrise des outils de planification tel que Microsoft Project serait un plus


Ce que nous offrons :


Entreprise en croissance, tous les jours de nouveaux projets et de grands défis.
Environnement de travail dynamique et international.
Attitude entrepreneuriale et propositions d’améliorations valorisées.
Entreprise familiale stable, développement stratégique sur le long terme.
Possibilité de formation interne ou externe.
Ambiance de travail agréable et esprit d’équipe.
Cadre de travail moderne, professionnel, lumineux et spacieux.
Idéalement situé entre montagne et lac.
Nombreux évènements intra et inter-entreprises.

Title: Real Estate Data & AI Analyst (m/w/d)
Description:
Du bist eine engagierte, motivierte und kundenorientierte Persönlichkeit, die bereit ist, in einer dynamischen und schnelllebigen Umgebung viel Verantwortung zu übernehmen? Du interessierst Dich für Immobilien, Daten und digitale Geschäftsmodelle? Dann bist Du genau die Verstärkung, die wir suchen!

Wir sind ein führendes Data & AI Startup, das Datenprodukte mithilfe innovativer Data-Science-Methoden entwickelt und Unternehmen auf ihrem Weg zur datengetriebenen Organisation begleitet. Zur Verstärkung unseres Teams suchen wir eine hochmotivierte Person, die uns im Bereich Real Estate Data & AI Analytics unterstützt und datenbasierte Lösungen für Immobilienunternehmen, Investoren und weitere Marktteilnehmer mitentwickelt.




Deine Aufgaben

Als Real Estate Data Analyst (m/w/d) bei Novalytica arbeitest Du an der Schnittstelle von Immobilienwirtschaft, Datenanalyse und Technologie. Du unterstützt unser Team bei der Beschaffung, Aufbereitung, Analyse und Visualisierung von Immobiliendaten und trägst dazu bei, datengetriebene Entscheidungsgrundlagen für unsere Kunden zu schaffen.

Zu Deinen Aufgaben gehören unter anderem:

Identifikation, Beschaffung und Aufbereitung relevanter Immobilien-, Markt- und Geodaten
Mitarbeit bei der Entwicklung und Weiterentwicklung datenbasierter Produkte und Analysemodelle
Datenprozessierung, Automatisierung und Qualitätssicherung mit Python und R
Unterstützung bei Kundenprojekten von der Konzeption bis zur Umsetzung
Entwicklung von Datenplattformen und Reporting-Lösungen, insbesondere im Microsoft Umfeld (Microsoft Fabric, Microsoft Power BI)
Ableitung von Handlungsempfehlungen aus Datenanalysen
Identifikation neuer Geschäftsmöglichkeiten im Bereich Real Estate Analytics




Dein Profil

2–4 Jahre Erfahrung in Data Analytics, Real Estate Analytics, Consulting oder einem verwandten Bereich
Abgeschlossenes Studium in einem relevanten Bereich wie Data Science, Wirtschaftsinformatik, Statistik, Geografie, Finanzen, Wirtschaftswissenschaften oder einem ähnlichen Fachgebiet
Interesse an Immobilienmärkten, Immobiliendaten, Portfoliomanagement und datengetriebenen Entscheidungsprozessen
Programmierkenntnisse in Python und R oder die Bereitschaft, diese weiterzuentwickeln
Sicherer Umgang mit modernen KI-Tools wie ChatGPT/Codex, Claude, Cursor, Copilot oder vergleichbaren Anwendungen zur Unterstützung von Datenanalyse und Code-Entwicklung
Erfahrung mit Business-Intelligence-Tools wie Power BI, Qlik Sense oder Tableau oder die Bereitschaft, diese zu erlernen
Sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse und fliessende Englischkenntnisse; Französisch wäre von Vorteil
Du bist engagiert, kreativ und strukturiert und bringst eine starke Hands-on-Mentalität mit
Du arbeitest gerne eigenverantwortlich, bist kundenorientiert und denkst unternehmerisch
Leidenschaft für Daten und ein gutes Verständnis für wirtschaftliche Zusammenhänge




Was wir bieten

Du wirst Teil eines dynamischen, wachsenden Data Science Unternehmen mit einem ehrgeizigen Team
Die Möglichkeit, mit modernen Technologien und Tools im Bereich Data Science, Analytics und Business Intelligence zu arbeiten
Ein spannendes Arbeitsumfeld an der Schnittstelle von Immobilienwirtschaft, Daten und Technologie
Eine flexible und kreative Arbeitsumgebung mit hochqualifizierten Mitarbeiter:innen
Die Möglichkeit, im Home-Office zu arbeiten
Mitarbeit an einer Vielzahl von Datenprojekten für verschiedene Kunden
Viel Eigenverantwortung und die Möglichkeit, eigene Ideen einzubringen
Direkter Kundenkontakt und Einblicke in strategische Fragestellungen der Immobilienbranche




Startdatum: Per sofort oder nach Vereinbarung

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Career Area

Technology, Digital and Data

Job Description

Your Work Shapes the World at Caterpillar Inc.

When you join Caterpillar, you're joining a global team who cares not just about the work we do – but also about each other. We are the makers, problem solvers, and future world builders who are creating stronger, more sustainable communities. We don't just talk about progress and innovation here – we make it happen, with our customers, where we work and live. Together, we are building a better world, so we can all enjoy living in it.

Job Summary

The Caterpillar Enterprise Strategy Group is seeking a Business Intelligence Analyst to pair quantitative techniques with business acumen, providing data-driven insight and analysis in support of our Rental & BCP Growth Plans with dealers. Primary roles/responsibilities for this position include, but are not limited to:


Utilizing multiple data sets throughout the enterprise and dealer network to identify data discrepancies, patterns, trends, and business insights.
Serve as a primary analytics partner to project teams, dealers, and cross‑functional stakeholders, ensuring analytical solutions directly support business decision‑making for Rental and BCP Growth projects.
Develop analytics tools and solutions in partnership with business stakeholders supporting dealer growth plans.
Translate complex analytical findings into clear, compelling narratives for enterprise leadership, dealer partners, and cross‑functional teams


What You Have


Intermediate to advanced Data Visualization (PowerBI) and Relational Database (SQL) skills
Exceptional communication skills with the ability to translate complex analytical insights into clear, actionable information for non-technical business audiences.
Extensive experience in Accuracy/Attention to Detail, Effective Communications, Business Data Analysis and Research Analysis
Working knowledge of Product/Solution Design Development, Technical Documentation and Requirements Analysis
Bachelor’s degree, preferably in business, marketing, economics, statistics, or a similar field with quantitative coursework.


Top Candidates Will Also Have


Prior experience in dealer-facing roles
Analytical programming experience (Microsoft Power Automate, Python etc)
Demonstrated Leadership attributes experience (Intellectual Curiosity, Good Business Skills/Judgement and Courage/Emotional Intelligence)


Additional Information


A valid Swiss work permit or Swiss or EU-25/EFTA citizenship is required for this position.
This role requires up to 10% of business travel time.


Posting Dates

Caterpillar is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Qualified applicants of any age are encouraged to apply

Not ready to apply? Join our Talent Community.

Job Description

Your Work Shapes the World at Caterpillar Inc.

When you join Caterpillar, you're joining a global team who cares not just about the work we do – but also about each other. We are the makers, problem solvers, and future world builders who are creating stronger, more sustainable communities. We don't just talk about progress and innovation here – we make it happen, with our customers, where we work and live. Together, we are building a better world, so we can all enjoy living in it.

Job Summary

The Caterpillar Enterprise Strategy Group is seeking a Business Intelligence Analyst to pair quantitative techniques with business acumen, providing data-driven insight and analysis in support of our Rental & BCP Growth Plans with dealers. Primary roles/responsibilities for this position include, but are not limited to:

  • Utilizing multiple data sets throughout the enterprise and dealer network to identify data discrepancies, patterns, trends, and business insights.
  • Serve as a primary analytics partner to project teams, dealers, and cross‑functional stakeholders, ensuring analytical solutions directly support business decision‑making for Rental and BCP Growth projects.
  • Develop analytics tools and solutions in partnership with business stakeholders supporting dealer growth plans.
  • Translate complex analytical findings into clear, compelling narratives for enterprise leadership, dealer partners, and cross‑functional teams

What You Have

  • Intermediate to advanced Data Visualization (PowerBI) and Relational Database (SQL) skills
  • Exceptional communication skills with the ability to translate complex analytical insights into clear, actionable information for non-technical business audiences.
  • Extensive experience in Accuracy/Attention to Detail, Effective Communications, Business Data Analysis and Research Analysis
  • Working knowledge of Product/Solution Design Development, Technical Documentation and Requirements Analysis
  • Bachelor’s degree, preferably in business, marketing, economics, statistics, or a similar field with quantitative coursework.

Top Candidates Will Also Have

  • Prior experience in dealer-facing roles
  • Analytical programming experience (Microsoft Power Automate, Python etc)
  • Demonstrated Leadership attributes experience (Intellectual Curiosity, Good Business Skills/Judgement and Courage/Emotional Intelligence)

Additional Information

  • A valid Swiss work permit or Swiss or EU-25/EFTA citizenship is required for this position.
  • This role requires up to 10% of business travel time.

Posting Dates

Caterpillar is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Qualified applicants of any age are encouraged to apply

Not ready to apply? Join our Talent Community.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
K&K Talents is an international recruiting agency that has been providing technical resources in the European region since 1993. This position is with one of our clients in Switzerland who is actively hiring candidates to expand their teams.

Position : Data Engineer

Location : Zurich, Switzerland(Hybrid)

Type : Permanent

Key responsibilities

• Design, build, and maintain ETL processes and data delivery pipelines for downstream applications.

• Create final data deliveries, primarily files, to be consumed by internal application teams.

• Work with databases, source data, transformation logic, and target delivery formats.

• Query databases, analyze source data, and optimize SQL or ETL logic where needed.

• Support data migration and integration activities within the ROC Core program.

• Work with internal application clients to understand data requirements, issues, and delivery expectations.

• Translate business and technical requirements into practical ETL and data delivery solutions.

• Investigate data quality issues, mapping questions, transformation logic, and delivery problems.

• Collaborate with software developers, business analysts, project managers, and upstream data providers.

• Contribute to documentation, data mappings, delivery specifications, and operational handover materials.




Required experience

• Strong experience as a Data Engineer, ETL Engineer, Integration Engineer, Migration Engineer, or similar role.

• Broad hands-on experience with ETL concepts, tools, and delivery patterns.

• Strong database experience, including SQL querying, analysis, and optimization.

• Experience working with data migrations, system integrations, or large-scale data transformation initiatives.

• Ability to understand data structures, mappings, dependencies, and transformation logic.

• Strong communication skills and ability to work directly with internal application stakeholders.

• Able to investigate problems independently and drive practical solutions.

• Comfortable working in a delivery-focused, high-stakes program environment.




Note: Applicants for employment in Switzerland should possess work authorization which does not require sponsorship by the employer for a visa.
